// How vetting works

We reject 94.3%
of applicants.

Every name on the platform cleared three gates — an uncheatable language test, two AI interviews, and a human recruiter review. This is the filter we'd want if we were hiring.

01

Uncheatable English test.

A 25-minute proctored assessment with face match, gaze tracking, and environment scan. Scores CEFR A1–C2 across fluency, grammar, pronunciation, and comprehension.

25 MIN · AUTO-SCORED
02

Two recorded AI interviews.

Role-specific scenarios evaluated by our assessment model and flagged for human review. Anti-cheat monitors for coaching, scripting, and multi-person attempts.

45 MIN · AI + HUMAN
03

Human recruiter review.

A senior recruiter reviews every interview tape, verifies two references by phone, and confirms ID + work history. Only then do they go live on the platform.

60–90 MIN · BY NAME
5.7%
Approval rate this quarter. 3,842 applications reviewed — 218 approved.
See the full vetting process →

// What clients say

Hires who
stayed.

We care less about first-month reviews than about the people still working together a year in. Here's a sample.

★★★★★
"We filled a senior React role in four days. The shortlist they sent had three candidates and we hired the first one we interviewed. She's still with us 14 months later."
SK
Sarah K.
Head of Engineering · Mint Labs
★★★★★
"I'd used Upwork for five years and always wasted a week sifting candidates. Atlas does that week for you. The first three profiles I opened were all hireable."
MT
Marcus T.
Founder · Arcline
★★★★★
"The 10% fee is real. No hidden markup on the hourly. No ballooning service fees. What they quote is what we pay."

// For candidates

Are you an
A-player?
Apply to join.

We're picky — 5.7% approval rate — but once you're in, you work with US, UK, AU, and CA clients who pay full rate. No platform fees, ever.

0% fees, forever
Keep 100% of your hourly rate. Clients pay the 10%.
US / UK / AU / CA clients
Serious companies with budgets who hire, not bargain.
Assigned recruiter support
A real human on your side when things get messy.
ID VerifiedEvery candidate, liveness-checked.
Reference checkedTwo phone calls per candidate.
Auto contractsGenerated the moment you hire.
Escrow protectedFunds held until work is confirmed.
72h dispute resolutionReal humans, fast turnaround.
// Common questions

Things
people ask.

Still have questions? Drop us a note and we'll answer within a day — often within an hour.

10% of the hourly or monthly rate you pay the candidate. No markup on their rate, no ballooning service fees, no hidden premium tier. It's disclosed on every contract before you sign.
The English test uses face match, gaze tracking, environment scan, and audio analysis. Interviews use the same anti-cheat stack plus coaching detection. Every tape is then reviewed by a human recruiter who verifies references by phone.
Median time-to-hire is 4 days. You browse the pool, shortlist, interview on the platform, and the contract auto-generates the moment you say yes. No procurement cycle required.
Within the first 14 days, we replace them free. After that, the 72-hour dispute process kicks in for any billed hours or deliverable issues. Escrow holds funds until both sides confirm.
Forty-two countries and counting. Argentina, Philippines, Poland, Kenya, South Africa, Mexico, India, Vietnam, Portugal, Brazil — wherever the talent is. Every candidate meets the same vetting bar regardless of location.
Yes. You can fund escrow upfront (recommended) or run post-paid invoicing. Funds only release after the candidate logs hours and you have a 7-day adjustment window to flag anything. Disputes get a real human reviewer within 72 hours.
No. Full profiles, bios, reviews, rates, scorecards, portfolios — all open. You only sign up when you want to watch a video intro, save a favorite, message, send a proposal, or book an interview.

Home/ Find Talent/ Valentina C.
SIGNUP REQUIRED
Video intro · 2m 14s
Recorded Nov 4, 2025 · auto-captioned
ID VERIFIED PHONE VERIFIED REFERENCE CHECKED ★ TOP 5%

Valentina Castillo

Senior Full-stack Developer
🇨🇴 Medellín, Colombia GMT−5 · 4h overlap with EST
$42/hr
Hire rate
40 hrs/wk
Available
96/100
Profile Strength
C2 · ES · PT
Languages
ID Verified Liveness + gov't ID
WhatsApp Verified +57 ••• ••• 4832
C2 English (CEFR)
98/100 Integrity score
Vetted by Priya Senior recruiter · Oct 28, 2025

About

I'm a senior full-stack engineer based in Medellín with nearly a decade of experience shipping React and Node applications for Series A–C companies in the US and UK. I care about the stuff that doesn't ship — thoughtful design reviews, clean git history, code that the next person can actually read — and the stuff that does: shipping often, instrumenting everything, and being honest about tradeoffs.

Most recently I led a four-person team at Aperture Logistics, where I rebuilt their customer portal from a legacy Rails app into a modern Next.js architecture. We cut page load times by 68% and reduced portal-related support tickets by half in the first quarter after launch.

I work async-first, write thorough tickets, and default to writing things down. Outside work I maintain a small open-source library for React form validation (2.3k GitHub stars), run long distances on weekends, and read way too much non-fiction.

EnglishC2 · Mastery SpanishNative PortugueseB1 · Intermediate

Work history

Aperture Logistics
May 2023 — Present
Senior Full-stack Engineer · Platform team lead

Led a four-person team through a full migration from a legacy Rails monolith to a Next.js + GraphQL architecture. Cut median page load from 3.8s to 1.2s. Drove hiring for two additional engineers, owned the platform roadmap, and ran weekly design reviews.

Employment confirmed
Mint Labs
Aug 2020 — Apr 2023
Full-stack Engineer

Shipped the initial version of the customer-facing analytics dashboard — React on the front, Node + Postgres on the back. Built the real-time cohort visualization that became the product's signature view. Left to take on a team-lead role.

Employment confirmed
Arcline
Jun 2018 — Jul 2020
Frontend Engineer · First engineering hire

Joined pre-seed as employee #3 and built the SaaS product frontend from an empty repo. Shipped the MVP in ten weeks, the first 50 customers in six months. Left when the team grew past 20 and I wanted deeper full-stack work.

Employment confirmed
Tulpa Studios
Feb 2016 — May 2018
Junior Developer · Agency

Agency work for Colombian and regional e-commerce clients. Shopify themes, small Rails apps, a lot of CSS. Graduated university during this time. The place I learned to ship.

Reference pending

Reviews 4 reviews · from clients who hired via Atlas

4.9 ★★★★★
34 reviews across 4 engagements
98%
Would hire again
100%
On-time delivery
Showing 4 of 34
SK
Sarah K. 🇺🇸
Head of Engineering · Mint Labs · Hired for full-stack work, Aug 2020–Apr 2023
★★★★★
WOULD HIRE AGAIN

Valentina is the rare engineer who can own a problem end-to-end without needing hand-holding. She joined us when the dashboard was a proof-of-concept and turned it into something customers actually relied on. Her code reviews were some of the most thoughtful I've seen. When she left to lead a team, the entire engineering org took her out for a farewell dinner. Easy recommendation.

OCT 14, 2023 Verified engagement
MT
Marcus T. 🇬🇧
Founder · Arcline · Hired as first frontend engineer, Jun 2018–Jul 2020
★★★★★
WOULD HIRE AGAIN

Brought Arcline from zero frontend to a polished SaaS product in 14 months. She writes the kind of code you actually want to maintain. More importantly she thinks like a product person — I'd show her a Figma file and she'd come back with three sharper questions than I'd thought to ask. I'd hire her again in a heartbeat, at almost any rate.

JUL 22, 2020 Verified engagement
AR
Ana R. 🇲🇽
COO · Ferry Logistics · Hired for short-term integration work, Feb 2024
★★★★★
WOULD HIRE AGAIN

Shipped a complex integration between our ERP and Shopify in three weeks — the previous vendor quoted us three months. She caught edge cases we hadn't even flagged. Communication was crisp, timelines were met, invoicing was clean. No notes.

Valentina's reply · Feb 28, 2024
Thanks Ana — working with Ferry was great, your internal docs were the best I've seen in a contract engagement. Happy to jump back in if you ever need follow-up work.
FEB 24, 2024 Verified engagement
DP
David P. 🇨🇦
Tech Lead · Signal Retail · Hired for 6-week engagement, Q1 2024
★★★★☆
WOULD HIRE AGAIN

Strong engineer. She occasionally over-engineers solutions for the scale we were operating at, which burned some time early on. But once we had a conversation about it she dialed back immediately and delivered exactly what we needed. Would work with her again on a larger-scope project.

Valentina's reply · Mar 15, 2024
Fair feedback, David — thanks for calling it out early. I took the lesson: match the solution to the scale of the problem, and confirm upfront. Appreciated working with you.
MAR 12, 2024 Verified engagement

// How it works

How it works.

Two walkthroughs. One platform. No surprises.

Whether you're hiring or being hired, here's exactly what happens from first click to first paycheck. No jargon, no fluff — just the mechanics.

Hiring in days,
not weeks.

Here's what happens from the moment you land on the platform to the moment your new hire starts working.

Average time to first hire · 3–5 days
01 OF 10
Instant

Browse freely. No account needed.

Search the full talent pool before you commit to anything. Filter by role, skills, budget, timezone, country, and more — no signup wall.

Search by role or specific skill
Filter by budget, timezone, English level, availability
Sort by Profile Strength, rate, or rating
Save favorites (requires signup)
Compare up to 4 candidates side-by-side (requires signup)
You'll see every candidate's photo, scorecard, bio, portfolio, reviews, and work history — all without an account.
Filter sidebar
Live results
02 OF 10
Instant

See everything that matters.

Every candidate has a full profile with verified work history, references, scorecards, portfolio, and reviews — all visible to you without signing up. Only video and voice intros are gated.

Read full bio and verified work history
See the full scorecard: English, role skill, soft skills, integrity
Browse work samples and portfolio in a lightbox
Read all reviews — on-platform + off-platform from US/UK/AU/CA clients
See verification badges: ID, phone, English, recruiter-vetted
Our recruiters verify every employment claim by phone. Reviews come from real clients — no fake profiles, no inflated ratings.
Identity verified
Full scorecard
34 real reviews
03 OF 10
2 minutes

Create a free account.

Sign up with email and password. You only need to verify your ID before your first hire — not before browsing, not before messaging, not before interviewing.

Email + password
Country of operation
What you're hiring for (pre-fills your searches)
Agree to Terms of Service
Your account is free forever. You only pay when you hire — 10% flat on top of the candidate's rate. That's it.
2-minute signup
04 OF 10
Variable

Two ways to start.

Message candidates directly if you've already found someone you like, or post a job and let your assigned recruiter bring you a shortlist.

Option A · Direct contact
Open any profile, click "Message"
Ask questions, request intro calls
Move them toward a proposal when ready
Option B · Post a job
Fill in role, skills, budget, hours, timezone, start date
Candidates matching 60%+ see your job
Your recruiter builds a top-5 shortlist in 24 hours
No bidding wars. No spam applications from people who don't match. Only candidates who actually fit the role.
05 OF 10
Within 24 hours

24-hour shortlist, handpicked.

A talent specialist assigned to your role category reviews your job and handpicks a top-5 shortlist from our vetted pool — with a written note on each candidate explaining why they chose them.

Email + in-app notification when your shortlist is ready
Review each candidate's full profile
Read your specialist's note explaining each pick
Shortlist sits alongside candidates who expressed interest on their own
Your specialist is a real person — not an algorithm. They know your role category and have interviewed the candidates personally.
Human-picked
Written note per pick
06 OF 10
Your schedule

Interview inside the platform.

No Zoom links, no scheduling back-and-forth. Open the candidate's availability calendar, pick a time that works, and the video call happens inside the platform at that time.

Click "Check availability & book interview" on any profile
Browse the candidate's open time slots, pick one
Join the built-in video call at the scheduled time — no external tools
Record the call, take private notes, invite teammates
After the call: Shortlist / Send Proposal / Pass / Book another
Recordings are yours to keep. Rewatch before deciding, or share with a business partner for a second opinion.
Live availability
Built-in call
07 OF 10
Usually same day

Negotiate rates in the chat.

The candidate sends a proposal with rate, hours, and start date. Accept, counter, or decline — all from the chat. Counter-offers can include scheduled rate changes to lock in a future raise.

Proposal appears as a card in your chat thread
Click into the full proposal view
Review rate, hours, start date, scheduled rate changes, cover note
Three actions: Accept · Counter · Decline
Full proposal history preserved in chat, forever
Everything is in writing. No "we agreed on what?" conversations later.
Proposal as chat card
Accept, counter, decline
08 OF 10
2 minutes to sign

Contract generates itself.

The second you accept a proposal, the platform generates a full legal contract with the agreed terms. Both parties sign inside the platform. Work can't start until both have signed.

Terms auto-pulled from proposal: rate, hours, start date, scheduled changes
Standard clauses: 7-day termination notice, confidentiality, IP ownership
Both parties e-sign — typed name + timestamp + IP log
Legally binding in US / UK / AU / CA
Stored in both dashboards, downloadable as PDF
No lawyer needed. No DocuSign subscription. Standard terms we've refined with employment lawyers in every market we serve.
Auto-generated
Both sign inside platform
09 OF 10
Automatic

Work starts. Payments handled.

For hourly work, the candidate's time tracker logs hours with periodic screenshots. For fixed-price, you fund escrow upfront. Either way, payments flow automatically on the schedule defined in the contract.

Hourly engagement
Candidate tracks hours via built-in tracker
Screenshots at random intervals (or auto-submit if you trust them)
7-day adjustment window before hours lock
Weekly auto-payment once hours lock
Fixed-price engagement
You fund escrow upfront
Funds release on milestone completion
Disputes routed to your specialist
You see exactly what you're paying for. No mystery invoices.
Hours & screenshots
Adjustment window
10 OF 10
Any time, forever

Your specialist is your backup.

The talent specialist who built your shortlist is your ongoing point of contact. If your hire goes MIA, if you want to renegotiate, if a dispute comes up — you message them, not a ticket queue.

Direct chat with your specialist always available
72-hour resolution SLA on disputes
Written decisions on every dispute, appealable to admin
Replacement assistance if the fit isn't right
Rate negotiations, contract amendments, anything else
You're not on your own after you hire. That's the whole point.
Direct line
⚡ Fast replies
// Pricing in context

What it costs you.

One fee. Ten percent. On top of the candidate's rate. That's the entire pricing model — no markup on their hourly, no ballooning service fees based on spend, no "premium" tier that's actually the only tier that works.

The math, in plain numbers.

Hire Maria at $10/hr for 40 hours a week. She takes home 100% of her rate. We get 10% on top — disclosed on every invoice, visible in every contract.

See the full pricing page →
Maria's rate$10/hr
× 40 hrs / week$400
Atlas fee (10%)+ $40
You pay / week$440
// When things go sideways

What if it doesn't work out?

We don't promise guarantees. Guarantees attract freelancers who game them — not the A-players we actually want. Here's what we do provide:

1 You see everything before hiring — video intro, voice intro, full scorecard, reviews, portfolio, references.
2 You interview on-platform and can record the call to rewatch before deciding.
3 Contracts allow 7-day no-penalty termination — either side can walk, no drama.
4 Your specialist helps find a replacement if the fit isn't right, from their own shortlist.
5 Dispute resolution within 72 hours with a written decision, appealable to admin.

No trial weeks. No money-back gimmicks. Just full transparency before the hire, and real human support if something changes.

// Client FAQ

Client questions.

The things people ask before hiring their first Atlas candidate.

Median time-to-hire is 4 days from posting a job. If you're messaging a candidate directly from Browse, it can be same-day.
Yes. Most of our candidates work 40 hrs/week for a single client long-term. The 10% fee applies whether it's 5 hours or 40.
No minimum. One-hour engagements are supported for short tasks, though most clients start with at least a week to see if the fit is right.
Absolutely. Some clients run a trial with two candidates for the first week and keep the better fit. The 10% fee applies to each active contract.
Their profile shows exactly when they'll be available next. You can send them a message expressing interest — they'll see it when they open up.
You message your specialist. They review both sides, issue a written decision within 72 hours, and funds release accordingly. Either side can appeal to admin.
Yes, after 12 months of on-platform work. Before then, there's a one-time conversion fee equal to 15% of the annualized rate. Disclosed upfront in every contract.
Yes. Browsing is free. Signup is free. Messaging is free. Posting a job is free. You only pay the 10% fee when you hire, and only on paid work hours. Payment processing is on us.

Ready to browse?

Browse Talent

From application
to first paycheck.

Here's every step of becoming an approved Atlas candidate. No hidden steps. No surprises. No bait-and-switch.

Typical time to go live · 7–14 days

Most applicants don't make it through. Here's exactly what it takes.

01 OF 10
2 minutes

Start your application.

Create a free candidate account. If a recruiter reached out with a referral link, use it — it credits them and changes nothing for you.

Legal name (as on your ID)
Email + password
Country
Role category you're applying for
Agree to Terms
Your application is always free. You'll never pay to apply, and you'll never pay to stay on the platform.
Free forever
2 minutes
02 OF 10
1 minute

Verify your phone on WhatsApp.

We use WhatsApp to keep in touch throughout your time on Atlas. Enter your number, receive a code on WhatsApp, enter it to verify.

Enter phone number with country code
Receive OTP code on WhatsApp
Enter code — auto-verified
WhatsApp only. If you don't use WhatsApp, you'll need to set it up to apply. It's how your recruiter will reach you later, too.
Message on WhatsApp
OTP in-app
03 OF 10
2–3 minutes

ID + liveness check.

Upload a government-issued ID. Then a quick liveness check where you turn your head and blink. This locks your face as your verified identity for every future interview.

Choose ID type: passport, national ID, driver's license
Photo of front (and back if applicable)
Liveness check: follow the prompts on screen
Face recognition matches your liveness to your ID
Your face becomes locked — all future interviews confirm it's you
This protects you too. Nobody else can impersonate you on the platform.
Your ID
Liveness match
04 OF 10
20–25 minutes

English assessment.

A CEFR-scored English test covering fluency, pronunciation, grammar, and comprehension. Webcam and screen recording on. No cheating — the anti-cheat system detects everything.

Pre-flight: webcam, mic, lighting, quiet environment, one screen
360° environment scan with your camera
Multiple choice, reading aloud, listening, short writing
Face, gaze, voice, behavior tracked throughout
Scored within minutes
⚠ Retake rules
1First fail: retake in 7 days with specific feedback
2Second fail without improvement: retake in 30 days
3Third fail: permanent rejection (appealable to admin)
We care about real English ability, not test-taking tricks. Be yourself.
Live question
Live anti-cheat feed
05 OF 10
20–30 minutes

First video interview.

A recorded AI-conducted video interview about your communication, problem-solving, and judgment. Same anti-cheat rules as the English test.

Pre-flight checks + environment scan
6–10 questions: behavioral, situational, rapid-fire
15–30 seconds to think, 60–120 seconds to answer
Dynamic follow-ups based on your answers
Recorded video + audio + screen
⚠ Retake rules
1First fail: retake in 7 days with feedback
2Second fail without improvement: retake in 30 days
3Third fail: permanent rejection
Your recruiter watches this personally. The AI score is one input — a human makes the final call.
AI asks
Timer, then answer
06 OF 10
Instant after passing Interview 1

Meet your recruiter.

You're assigned to one of our 11 recruiters. They review all your interviews, approve your profile, support you after you go live, and handle any disputes. Your recruiter is yours forever — not per-job.

See your recruiter's photo, name, bio, and languages
Direct chat with them anytime
They review and approve your full profile before you go live
They're your first point of contact for anything that comes up
Your recruiter is your advocate. If something isn't fair, they're the first person to ask.
Yours forever
Direct chat
07 OF 10
30–45 minutes

Role-specific interview.

The deep one. Role-specific scenarios, skill tests, and (for some roles) live tasks — inbox triage, cold-call roleplay, system design, bookkeeping problems. Same anti-cheat rules.

Pre-flight + environment scan
Role-specific question set (different for each category)
Some roles include live problem-solving or screen-share
Scored on a role-specific rubric
⚠ Retake rules
1First fail: retake in 7 days with feedback
2Second fail without improvement: retake in 30 days
3Third fail: permanent rejection
Apply for multiple role categories? You'll take Interview 2 once per category.
Live task
Live scoring
08 OF 10
30–60 minutes

Build your profile.

Fill in your bio, rate, availability, skills, work history, portfolio, and references. Your recruiter reviews it and either approves it or asks you to revise.

Professional photo, bio, rate, max hours/week
Skills, tools, education, certifications
Up to 5 previous employers with contacts for verification
Up to 10 work samples / portfolio pieces
Live preview — see your profile as clients will
Submit for recruiter review
References are optional but push you higher in search. Only US/UK/AU/CA references can leave public reviews.
Live preview
Your edits
09 OF 10
5–10 minutes

Record your intro.

A 75-second video intro and a 60-second voice intro. Structured prompts guide you through. One retake allowed. The face and voice must match your verified identity.

Read the structured prompt: who you are, what you do, what you want
Pre-flight checks before recording
Record video (one take, one retake)
Record voice (one take, one retake)
Identity-lock check runs silently
Clients watch your intro before anything else. This is the #1 thing that gets you hired.
Structured prompts
Identity lock live
10 OF 10
Ongoing

You're live.

Your profile goes live. Clients find you in search, watch your intro, read your reviews, and hire you. You get paid weekly for hourly work, on milestones for fixed-price — always at 100% of your rate.

Visible to all clients in Browse and search
One-click "Express Interest" on matching jobs
Direct messages, proposals, interviews, signed contracts — all in-platform
Weekly pay in your local currency or USD — your choice
Build reviews that become your reputation
Re-certify every 3 months to boost scores (optional, paid)
Your earnings are yours. 0% platform fee, forever.
You're live
One-click apply
// How we catch cheaters

How we make sure
nobody cheats.

Our anti-cheat system is the strictest in the industry. Here's exactly how it works — and why you shouldn't be scared of it if you're genuine.

ID + liveness capture locks your face at application. That face becomes your verified identity for the life of the account.
Every future interview matches you against the locked face in real-time, using the same face recognition that unlocks modern phones.
Someone else sitting the interview? Instantly flagged. The session is paused and routed to a human reviewer before any score is recorded.
Gaze tracking watches where you're looking. Looking off-screen for a few beats during a tough question is normal — we're not flagging humans being human.
Repeatedly glancing at a second screen? That's a flag. Especially if your eyes track the same off-screen area during every question.
Head pose tracked continuously. Turning away to read from a different device is obvious to the model and gets surfaced for human review.
Voice fingerprint matched throughout. The voice we hear has to be the same voice that recorded the voice intro — and the same voice that answered question 1.
Background voice detection catches whisperers and coaches. Someone off-camera feeding answers? The mic picks up more than you'd expect.
Lip-sync correlation checks your mouth matches the audio. Dubbed or pre-recorded answers played through speakers don't correlate with mouth movement. Obvious to the model.
360° room scan before the test starts. We see what's on your desk, your walls, and anyone else in the room. Flagged items: other people, phones within reach, second monitors.
Screen recording of your device throughout. Every window, every tab switch, every focus change is captured and reviewed.
Virtual camera tools (OBS, ManyCam) blocked at the browser. If we detect you're feeding a recorded or composited video as your "live" camera, the session ends immediately.
Response timing analyzed. ChatGPT-paraphrased answers have a tell — the pause-then-deliver cadence is distinct from how humans actually think and talk.
Answer style analyzed for LLM patterns. We have classifiers that flag the kind of "consultant-speak," structure-heavy, vocabulary-inflated responses that LLMs love to produce.
Rapid-fire questions designed so there's no time to look anything up. 10-second response windows on certain questions. If you're reading from a screen, you miss the window. If you know your stuff, you answer easily.

"If you're genuine, the system is invisible. If you're trying to game it, you'll get caught."

Your recruiter reviews every flagged moment personally. False positives happen occasionally and we review them with you, by name, on a call.

// If the answer is no

What if I fail or get rejected?

We'll tell you, specifically. No mystery rejections, no "we went with another candidate" boilerplate. The most common scenarios:

1 Failed English test or Interview 1/2: 7-day cooldown with specific feedback. Second fail without improvement: 30-day cooldown. Third fail: permanent rejection (appealable).
2 Profile revision requested: Your recruiter tells you exactly what to fix. Revise and resubmit — no penalty, no retake delay.
3 Permanent rejection: You'll know why. Appeals go to admin and are reviewed within 7 days by a human who wasn't involved in the original decision.
4 Common rejection reasons: weak spoken English, thin work history with no verifiable references, inconsistent face/voice between stages, cheating signals detected, portfolio that doesn't match claimed skills.
// Zero-fee promise

What you earn.

You keep 100% of your rate. Always. Forever. The client pays a 10% platform fee on top of your rate — you never pay us a cut of what you earn.

100%
Of your rate

You set your hourly rate. When the client pays, that full amount flows to you — no deductions, no hidden service fees, no "payment processing" skim.

0%
Platform fee

You never pay Atlas anything to work through us. Not to apply, not to stay on, not to get paid. The client pays our 10% on top of your rate.

$20–50
Re-certification (optional)

Want to re-test after 3 months to boost your scores? Optional. Small fee covers the AI interview infrastructure. Skip it and nothing changes.

// Getting paid

How you get paid.

Payments flow automatically, on the schedule in your contract. No invoicing, no chasing, no "sorry the wire didn't go through." If you're tracking hours, payment happens when hours lock; if you're on milestones, payment releases when the client approves.

• Weekly for hourly work. On milestones for fixed-price.
• Your local currency or USD — your choice. Rates lock at proposal signing, not at payout.
• Payouts via Wise, directly inside the platform. No external account to set up, no separate dashboard to maintain.
• First payout is held 3 business days as a one-time security check. After that, payments are instant on schedule.
// Candidate FAQ

Candidate questions.

The questions applicants ask most, answered honestly.

Typical: 7–14 days from applying to being live on the platform. Depends on how quickly you schedule your interviews and build your profile. Some applicants go live in 4 days; a few take 3 weeks.
7-day cooldown with feedback. Fail again: 30 days. Fail a third time: permanent rejection — appealable to admin within 7 days.
Yes — anytime. Toggle "Away" in your settings and you disappear from search. Active contracts continue, new inquiries pause. Come back whenever.
Your profile shows when you'll next be available. Clients can still message you expressing interest; you can respond when you have capacity.
Yes. You do Interview 2 (the role-specific one) once per category. English test and Interview 1 are one-time across the whole platform.
No. Applying is free. Staying on the platform is free. Getting paid is free. The only optional fee is re-certification ($20–50) if you choose to re-test for higher scores.
Not mandatory. After 3 months you can re-take any assessment to potentially bump your scores — useful if you've improved substantially since your first attempt. Skip it and nothing changes.
Anywhere. Most clients are US/UK/AU/CA, but we have clients in EU, LATAM, and MENA. You set your own availability, timezone, and rate.
Your recruiter represents you. They review both sides, issue a written decision within 72 hours, and either party can appeal to admin. You're never alone in a dispute.
Yes, after 12 months of on-platform work with a given client. Before that, the client pays a conversion fee if they want to take you off-platform. Applies to them, not you — your earnings are never affected.
Do great work. Clients get prompted to review after every engagement ends. US/UK/AU/CA clients can leave public reviews; other clients' feedback is visible to your recruiter but not publicly displayed.

Think you have what it takes?

Only 5.7% of applicants are accepted. If you're A-player material, we want to meet you.

See Full Requirements

We think the best way to trust a platform is to see what the other side sees.

Looking for A-players?

Browse the full talent pool with no signup. Hire your first person in 3–5 days. 10% flat on top of their rate, no other fees.

// For working

Think you can hang?

5.7% acceptance rate. 0% fee on your earnings. Clients in the US/UK/AU/CA. Your recruiter is yours forever. Apply when you're ready.

// Pricing

Simple pricing.
No hidden fees.

Ten percent on top of the candidate's rate. Zero percent from the candidate. That's the entire pricing model — no tiers, no premium plans, no per-seat charges, no Connects, no markup hiding inside the hourly rate.

10%
Flat — clients
0%
Forever — candidates
$0
To browse & apply
Zero
Hidden fees
// For clients hiring

What you pay

10% flat, on top of the candidate's rate

One fee, always visible, always the same. If the candidate quotes $30/hr, you pay $33/hr total. No surcharge tiers, no enterprise plans, no annual commitments.

What's included
  • Unlimited browsing, no paywalls
  • Unlimited job posts
  • Assigned talent specialist + human-picked shortlists within 24 hours
  • In-platform video interviews with recording
  • Auto-generated contracts, legally binding in US/UK/AU/CA
  • Escrow or post-paid payments via Wise
  • 72-hour dispute resolution with written decisions
  • Replacement assistance if the fit isn't right
Browse Talent
// For candidates working

What you pay

0% forever, on every hour you work

You keep 100% of your rate. Forever. The client's 10% is on top of your quoted hourly — never taken out of your pocket. No Connects, no paid tier to get noticed, no cut of what you earn.

What's included
  • Free to apply, free to stay on the platform forever
  • Weekly pay in your local currency or USD via Wise
  • Assigned recruiter who reviews, approves, and represents you
  • Auto-contracts that protect your IP and payment terms
  • Public reviews from US/UK/AU/CA clients
  • No "Connects," no paid tier to get noticed
  • Optional re-certification ($20–50) to retest for higher scores
  • Dispute advocacy from your recruiter, always

// Pricing FAQ

Pricing questions.

The things people ask before hiring their first person, and before applying.

No. Clients pay 10% flat on top of the candidate's rate. Candidates pay 0%. Browsing, messaging, posting jobs, contracts, escrow, payment processing, and dispute resolution are all included. The only optional fee anywhere on the platform is candidate re-certification ($20–50) if they choose to retest.
Only on paid work hours. No monthly fees, no signup fees, no annual commitments. If you hire someone for 10 hours in a week, you're charged on those 10 hours. If you don't hire anyone, you pay nothing — the account stays free forever.
No minimum. Hire someone for 1 hour or 40 hours — the 10% is the same. No contract length minimums, no monthly caps, no "only if you spend $X per quarter" tiers.
Upwork's headline number looks similar, but it's stacked: client marketplace fee plus contract initiation fees plus Connects costs passed through to you via higher candidate rates plus premium memberships. Atlas is 10%. Period. No stacking.
Because asking the worker to subsidize the platform is how you end up with Upwork and Fiverr — where candidates cut corners, inflate rates to compensate, or churn to off-platform work. We want A-players to stay, get paid in full, and build reputation over time. Our unit economics work with clients alone.
After 12 months of on-platform work with a candidate, you can take them off-platform with no fee. Before 12 months, there's a one-time conversion fee equal to 15% of the annualized rate. This is spelled out in every contract — no surprises.
Atlas. Payment processing, international wire fees, currency conversion through Wise — all covered by us, out of the 10% client fee. Candidates get exactly what their contract says they'll get. Clients pay exactly what the calculator says they'll pay.
No. 10% is 10% whether you hire one person or a hundred. We don't do volume discounts because the main cost at scale is talent specialist support — and we don't want to quietly cut that support to make margin on volume deals.
Never. Profile Strength and search ranking are earned only — through test scores, interview performance, profile completeness, reviews, and "would hire again" rates. There's no paid tier, no "boost," no advertising on candidate profiles. Period.
US clients hiring US-based candidates get 1099-NECs from Atlas at year-end for anything paid through the platform. International candidates are responsible for their local tax obligations — we provide annual earnings summaries on request. Talk to your accountant for your specific situation.

// The bar is real
5.7%
of applicants get approved

We're honest about this because it's better for everyone. The A-players on the platform are A-players because we don't let everyone in. If you're genuinely strong, this protects your rate, your reputation, and your match with serious clients.

Why people most commonly get rejected
  1. Weak spoken EnglishConversational fluency isn't enough. Clients expect C1+ — comfortable business conversation, not tourist English.
  2. Thin work history, no verifiable referencesClaims on your profile need backing from real past employers. We call every reference.
  3. Inconsistent face or voice between stagesIf the person in Interview 1 doesn't match Interview 2 or the video intro, identity-lock flags trigger human review.
  4. Cheating signals during interviewsLLM-generated answers, off-screen coaching, second monitors, virtual cameras — all detected and flagged.
  5. Portfolio doesn't match claimed skillsIf you say you're senior React but your test scores and portfolio don't show it, the gap gets noticed.
// Ready?

Start your application.

Once all 6 boxes are checked and you've picked a role category, the button below unlocks. Applications are always free. You can always reapply after a cooldown if this isn't the right time.

Check all 6 boxes and pick a category
// A-Player Report

Quarterly data, from a year of hiring's hardest problems.

Every quarter, the Atlas research team publishes a free data report on what we've learned from processing 100k+ applications and running thousands of vetted hires. No gated PDFs, no form walls.

// Latest · Q4 2025

+38%
Y/Y growth in LATAM senior-dev supply on platform, Q4 2024 → Q4 2025

// Q1 2026

// Quarterly

Get next quarter's report
before it goes public.

One email per quarter. The finished report, in your inbox, a week before we publish it anywhere else. No marketing bait, no cross-sells, no "we thought you'd be interested in" follow-ups.

One email per quarter · Unsubscribe in one click
● Q4 2025 Published Nov 14, 2025 18 min read

The new supply map: where the A-players are coming from.

Over the last four quarters, the composition of the global senior-dev talent pool has shifted more dramatically than any year since 2020. We ran the numbers from 43,000 applications across 38 countries — here's what the data actually says.

PR
MH
TD
Atlas Research Team Priya Raman, Marcus Holt, Tomasz Dąbrowski
In this report
Section 01

The new supply map.

In Q4 2024, 61% of senior-dev applicants on Atlas came from Eastern Europe and South Asia. One year later, that number is 47%. Not because the region shrank — raw volume from EU-East and South Asia grew in absolute terms — but because other regions grew much faster.

The net effect is a more evenly distributed supply map than at any point in the platform's history. LATAM, Southeast Asia, and MENA now represent 41% of senior-dev applications combined, up from 26% a year ago.

Fig. 01 · Regional distribution
Share of senior-dev applications by region, Q4 2024 vs Q4 2025.
Source: Atlas applications data, 43,127 senior-dev submissions · N = applications, not approvals

The movement isn't random. Three drivers are doing most of the work: currency, infrastructure, and the collapse of remote-work skepticism in mid-market companies. We'll look at each.

Section 02

What they charge.

The most striking finding from this quarter isn't the supply growth — it's rate convergence. For the first time, median senior-dev rates from LATAM on Atlas are within 8% of what the same level of engineer charges off-platform in the Bay Area.

This isn't inflation on the LATAM side — their rates have held relatively stable in USD terms. It's that the Bay Area median for comparable senior engineers (5–8 YOE, full-stack, remote-willing) has compressed as more companies treat remote hiring as default.

Fig. 02 · Rate convergence
Median hourly rate, senior full-stack developer — Q4 2025.
Source: Atlas contract data Q4 2025 · Bay Area off-platform median from third-party sample, N=180 · Senior defined as 5-8 YOE full-stack
"The LATAM–Bay Area compression is the headline. But the quieter story is that EU-East is no longer the cheap option — it's the median option."
— Priya Raman, Atlas Research Team
Section 03

Why now.

The shift happened in 2025 because three things converged in the same calendar year. Any one of them alone would have produced marginal change; together they produced the largest supply-side migration we've measured.

+19%
USD strength vs BRL, MXN, and ARS median — 2025 drove LATAM candidate earnings up in local terms
82%
Of surveyed mid-market companies now treat "fully remote" as the default policy for engineering hires
3.4×
Growth in Latin American co-working + home fiber access since 2022 per World Bank infrastructure data

Currency, not charity.

A $50/hr rate paid to a Buenos Aires developer in 2023 bought one amount of rent, groceries, and savings. The same $50 in 2025 buys meaningfully more. This isn't a moral story — it's a straightforward USD-vs-local-currency math. It's also not sustainable forever, and several of our LATAM candidates now raise rates annually to track dollar strength.

The infrastructure tail caught up.

As recently as 2022, stable 50+ Mbps fiber was a scarcity item in much of LATAM and SEA outside major capitals. That's no longer true. Our Q4 candidate-infrastructure survey shows median download speeds in our LATAM cohort at 93 Mbps, with 81% on fiber. In 2023 those numbers were 38 Mbps and 52%.

RTO didn't win.

The 2023 Return-to-Office narrative was largely a Fortune 500 story. The mid-market — companies between 50 and 500 employees — didn't follow. Our Q3 2024 report documented this. Two years in, it's more definitive: the mid-market is remote-default, and that's where most Atlas clients hire from.

// Take this home

Get the full PDF in your inbox.

Section 04

The migration, quarter by quarter.

Aggregate numbers hide the sequencing. Here's the same supply data broken out by quarter across the last eight — the pattern makes the shift visible.

Fig. 03 · Quarterly trend
Senior-dev applications by region, indexed to Q1 2024 = 100.
Source: Atlas applications data, Q1 2024 – Q4 2025 · Indexed to each region's Q1 2024 baseline at 100

The LATAM line isn't a smooth curve — it hockey-sticks between Q4 2024 and Q1 2025. That's the quarter USD strength against BRL/ARS accelerated, and the quarter several LATAM-based remote-work communities (particularly Remotar in Brazil and NómadaTalent in Mexico) reached critical mass. The two effects compounded.

EU-East's flatness is the other half of the story. That region isn't shrinking — volume is up nominally — but it's not gaining share. The ceiling is real, and most of it is USD-denominated rate competition from regions where dollars go further.

Section 05

What this means for 2026.

Three practical things, for clients and for candidates:

For clients: cast wider nets.

If your last LATAM hire was in 2022 and you priced accordingly, your comps are stale. Senior LATAM full-stack engineers are now charging $85–100/hr on-platform and getting it. The cost advantage is still real vs Bay Area, but it's not the 3× discount it was two years ago — it's more like 15–25%.

For clients: the new supply isn't evenly distributed.

Growth is concentrated in four LATAM countries (Brazil, Argentina, Mexico, Colombia) and three SEA countries (Vietnam, Philippines, Indonesia). MENA growth is real but from a small base. Don't expect the same abundance out of every region.

For candidates: the compression is a rate ceiling, not a floor.

The LATAM–Bay Area convergence says that top-of-market LATAM senior rates are now near Bay Area medians. But median LATAM rates are still well below that. If you're in the top 10% of your local market, this is an opportunity to quote like it. If you're in the middle, don't pretend to be at the top — clients check.

Next quarter's report looks at the candidate side of this migration: where the people charging these rates live, what they say they want, and what's changing in their own economics. Q1 2026 publishes February.

More from the A-Player Report

All issues →
// The A-Player Journal

Hiring, working,
and winning.

Field notes from the Atlas research team on the global talent economy — who's getting hired, who's charging what, what's breaking, and what's actually working.

/

Home / Journal / Hiring Guides / The complete guide to hiring a VA in 2026
Hiring Guides

The complete guide to hiring a virtual assistant in 2026.

Pricing, sourcing, vetting, and working with a VA who actually moves the needle — not one you have to babysit. Everything we've learned from processing 14,000+ VA applications.

PR Priya Raman
·
Nov 18, 2025 Updated Nov 19 · 12 min read
● The complete guide
14,000
applications
Issue 47 · Based on 14,127 VA applications processed between Jan 2024 & Oct 2025
In this article

What a VA actually is in 2026.

The term "virtual assistant" covers a range of roles that aren't really the same job. Some VAs manage your inbox and calendar. Others run your entire operations stack — CRM maintenance, vendor invoicing, bookkeeping coordination, light project management. A few specialize so deeply in one vertical (real estate, health tech, e-commerce) that they're effectively industry analysts with an admin title.

When founders complain that "VAs don't work," they usually mean the VA they hired didn't match the scope they needed. A $600/mo generalist can't run an executive's inbox. A $2,500/mo senior EA will burn out on data entry they're overqualified for. Most VA failures aren't bad hires — they're bad matches.

i
A note on language

In this guide, "VA" means the generalist end of the spectrum. For specialized senior EAs, project managers, and operators, see the operator hiring guide (link swaps once we publish it).

When you should (and shouldn't) hire one.

Hiring a VA works best when you have a clear, repeating set of tasks you can offload and a small budget to match. The fastest-growing VA category in 2025 wasn't executive support — it was operations support for small e-commerce brands, fractional founders, and professional services firms.

Hire one if you find yourself:

  • Spending 10+ hours a week on scheduling, inbox, or data entry
  • Losing deals because you can't follow up on leads in under 24 hours
  • Running a business out of 40 tabs and 14 post-it notes
  • Willing to document how you work, not just what you want done

Don't hire one if:

  1. You don't have written standard operating procedures (SOPs) for the tasks you want to offload
  2. The work requires deep business context that takes 3+ months to acquire (that's a Chief of Staff hire, not a VA)
  3. You're hiring mainly to signal to yourself that you're growing (be honest about this one)

How much to pay.

This is the question founders ask first, which is the wrong order. The right question is: what scope will I give a VA, and what's that worth to my business? Then match to a tier. But since you're here anyway, here's the landscape.

Pricing tiers, compared.

Three rough bands across the global market in Q4 2025, based on 14,000+ applications we've processed:

Tier Monthly cost Typical scope Typical region
Entry $400 – $900 Data entry, scheduling, basic research, simple inbox triage Philippines, Kenya, Pakistan
Mid $1,100 – $2,000 Full inbox/calendar, CRM maintenance, light PM, vendor coordination LATAM (Mexico, Colombia, Argentina), Eastern Europe
Senior $2,200 – $4,000 Executive support, ops leadership, fractional COO-lite LATAM, Eastern Europe, select specialists in SEA

These are full-time-equivalent monthly rates. Part-time arrangements are proportionally cheaper but not cleanly linear — a strong VA working 10 hours a week often costs more than 25% of full-time, because the best ones have full dance cards.

// Looking to hire now?

Browse 400+ pre-vetted VAs on Atlas.

Every one English-tested, reference-checked, and interviewed. 10% flat fee. No gating to browse.

Browse VAs

Where the best VAs are coming from.

For a long time the answer to "where do I find a VA?" was functionally one word: the Philippines. Filipino VAs built the modern category — high English fluency, strong service culture, low overhead, deep talent density from a decade of BPO infrastructure. That's all still true in 2026.

What's changed is the rise of LATAM and Eastern Europe as serious VA regions, especially for US clients who want timezone overlap. A Buenos Aires or Mexico City VA is online during the full US workday; a Manila VA is not. For roles that require real-time coordination (inbox triage during business hours, live research during meetings), the timezone alignment is worth 15–25% in rate differential.

"The best VA we ever hired lived in Medellín, billed $1,800/mo, and made my Head of Ops redundant within a quarter. The best VA we ever lost billed $600/mo, lived 13 hours ahead, and quit because we couldn't find overlap."
— A client we interviewed for this piece

What to look for in an application.

Most applications look similar at first glance. The ones that actually work out share specific traits that don't show up on a resume but do show up in how they write.

Red flags.

  1. Generic cover letter. If the opener works for any job, skip it.
  2. Perfect grammar on cover letter, weak grammar in video intro. Means someone helped write the text. Trust the video.
  3. Claims "expert" at 10+ tools. Nobody is expert at everything. This is noise.
  4. No questions. Good operators have questions about scope, context, tools. Applications with zero questions are usually templated.
  5. Responds too fast. A thoughtful application takes time. Apps that arrive in under 20 minutes rarely hold up.

Green flags.

  • References a specific part of your job post
  • Asks one pointed clarifying question
  • Notes one concern or gap in their own background honestly
  • Includes a link — portfolio, Loom walkthrough, past client testimonial, anything
  • Writes in a voice that sounds like them, not a template
✓
Practical tip

Put a small instruction in your job post that's easy to miss — "include the word 'tuesday' somewhere in your application" — and only interview people who followed it. This alone filters out 60% of templated responses.

PDF
The 90-Day VA Onboarding Plan (template)
Week-by-week plan with check-ins, SOPs to document, and a 60-day review script. Used by 800+ Atlas clients.

The 90-day plan.

The first 90 days decide whether a VA works out. More hires fail in week 2 than in month 6. Here's the rhythm we recommend — and include as a template in the PDF above.

Days 1–14: Shadowing, watching, documenting. They don't do work — they watch how you work and write it down.

Days 15–45: Supervised execution. They do the work, you review it daily. Painful, necessary.

Days 46–90: Independent execution with weekly checkpoints. If you're still reviewing daily at day 60, one of you is the problem.

Common mistakes.

  1. Hiring before documenting SOPs. Asking someone to do tasks you've never written down is an expensive way to write them down.
  2. Hiring too cheap. A $400/mo VA you have to micromanage costs more than a $1,500/mo VA who runs themselves.
  3. Not having a probation period. 30 days is not enough. 90 is about right. Set it, say it out loud, and use it.
  4. Skipping the reference call. You'd be shocked how many red flags come out of a 15-minute call with someone's last manager.
// Every Tuesday

One great piece on hiring, every week.

No spam. No fluff. Read by 12,000+ founders & operators.

When to let them go.

This is the part of the guide nobody writes and everyone needs. Most founders hold onto a mediocre VA for 6 months longer than they should, because firing feels harder than tolerating. It isn't. It feels that way only until you've done it once.

!
If you're reading this wondering whether to let them go

You probably already have the answer. The question is whether you trust yourself enough to act on it. Our recruiters can walk you through the conversation and the handoff — that's part of what the 10% fee covers.

Signs it's time:

  • You're still reviewing day-to-day work at day 90
  • You're mentally rehearsing feedback conversations more than once a week
  • You avoid giving them hard tasks because you don't trust the output
  • You've described the role as "a work in progress" to three different people

The fastest way to recover from a bad VA hire is a clean ending and a replacement, not another month of hoping. Atlas offers free replacement assistance within the first 90 days — not because it's generous, but because it's the unit economics of hiring done right.

● Too long?

Here's what matters.

  • Most VA failures are scope mismatches, not bad people
  • $400/mo VAs and $2,500/mo VAs do different jobs — match to scope, not budget
  • LATAM is the fastest-growing region for US clients needing timezone overlap
  • Red flags: generic cover, perfect grammar + weak video, no questions, too fast
  • 90-day plan: 14 days watching, 30 days supervised, 45 days independent
  • If you're debating whether to let them go, you already know the answer

This article is updated quarterly as the VA landscape changes. Last major revision: Nov 18, 2025. Minor edit Nov 19 for regional pricing.

Home / Help Center / Payments & invoicing / How payments work on Atlas
Payments & invoicing

How payments work on Atlas.

4 min read · Updated 3 weeks ago · Reviewed by Payments Team
● Short version You're charged weekly for the hours your candidate worked, Atlas takes a 10% platform fee, and the candidate receives the rest within 48 hours of your payment clearing.

The billing cycle.

Atlas bills on a Monday-to-Sunday cycle. Every Monday morning at 8 AM UTC, you'll receive an invoice for the previous week's approved hours. A quick walkthrough of the cadence:

  • Candidates submit hours at the end of each week (Friday or over the weekend)
  • You have until Monday 8 AM UTC to review, approve, or flag them
  • Approved hours are billed Monday; unapproved ones auto-approve Tuesday 8 AM UTC unless flagged
  • Flagged hours are paused and routed to our support team for review (usually within 24 hours)
i
First invoice timing

Your first invoice may feel delayed — if your candidate starts mid-week, you won't be billed until the following Monday. After that, billing is weekly and predictable.

Payment methods.

We accept four payment methods, with bank transfer (ACH/SEPA) being our preferred method because it's both free and faster than wire.

Method Processing fee Settlement Best for
ACH (US) Free 2–3 business days Most US clients
SEPA (EU) Free 1–2 business days Most EU clients
Credit card 2.9% + $0.30 Instant One-off or urgent payments
Wire $25 per transfer 1–5 business days Large international transfers

You can add multiple methods to your account and set one as the backup — if your primary fails, we'll attempt the backup before notifying you.

When you're charged.

By default, you're charged the same Monday the invoice is generated. If your primary payment method fails, here's what happens in order:

  1. We attempt your backup method (if you've added one)
  2. If that also fails, we email you within 2 hours of the failure with details
  3. New bookings for that candidate are paused until the invoice is resolved
  4. You have 7 days to resolve before we flag the account — no late fees for bank-side issues
✓
Avoid surprise failures

Add at least two payment methods. Set the free method (ACH/SEPA) as primary and the card as backup. This is the configuration 92% of our clients end up using.

How candidates get paid.

Candidates are paid within 48 hours of your payment clearing. Atlas holds the funds in an insured escrow account between receipt and candidate payout — which is how we can guarantee on-time payments even if your bank pulls something back later.

For instant-settlement methods (card), we release funds after a 48-hour review window to protect against fraudulent charges. For bank methods (ACH/SEPA), candidates typically receive funds within 24 hours of your payment clearing.

Full flow, by method.

Method You → Atlas Atlas → Candidate Total
Credit card Instant +48 hrs ~48 hours
ACH 2–3 days +24 hrs 3–4 days
SEPA 1–2 days +24 hrs 2–3 days
Wire 1–5 days +24 hrs 2–6 days

Disputes and refunds.

If the work delivered doesn't match what was agreed, you can open a dispute within 14 days of the invoice. Atlas holds the relevant funds in escrow during the dispute — so nothing moves until the dispute is resolved.

Refunds for approved work are rare but possible. They typically require mutual agreement between you and the candidate, or a dispute ruling in your favor. Either way, the process is documented in When and how to open a dispute.

!
Chargebacks are different from disputes

If you ask your bank for a chargeback instead of opening an Atlas dispute, we have to freeze the escrow and the candidate's account pending resolution — which can take 60–90 days. An Atlas dispute is almost always faster.

What about taxes?

You'll receive:

  • A real invoice per payment (PDF), matching what was charged
  • A year-end summary of total paid, broken down by candidate and category
  • VAT/GST is not charged by Atlas — check with your local tax authority for your obligations

Candidates handle their own taxes in their country of residence. Atlas does not withhold taxes from their payments. If you're a US client and your candidate is US-based, we'll send a 1099 if applicable — for international candidates, this doesn't apply.

Still have questions?

Check the related articles in the sidebar, or contact support using the block below. Our average first-response time on payment questions is under 10 minutes during business hours.

Was this helpful? Your feedback helps us improve this guide.
● What could be clearer?
Sent anonymously to our support team

Still stuck on a payment question?

Chat with our support team — average 10-minute response time on payment tickets.

Related articles
// Trust & Safety · how we protect both sides

The anti-cheat
platform for remote work.

Every candidate is verified. Every contract is escrowed. Every dispute goes to a real human mediator. Fraud on Atlas isn't just discouraged — it's mechanically hard.

5.7%
Applicant acceptance rate · 2025
$0
Fraud losses to clients · 2025
5–7 days
Avg dispute resolution time
100%
Payments held in insured escrow
● The anti-cheat stack · candidate side

We verify humans,
not profiles.

Most platforms take a candidate's word that they are who they say. We don't. Every Atlas candidate passes through three primary verification phases before their profile goes live — and a continuous anti-cheat layer during every test and video interview.

Phase 01 · Identity

Government ID
+ liveness check.

Every applicant uploads a government-issued photo ID and completes a 3-angle liveness capture — looking straight, left, and up. We verify the ID is authentic (hologram, microtext, issuing authority) and that the face in the ID matches the person in front of the camera, in real time.

This kills the two most common fraud patterns in outsourcing: ghost profiles (one person applying as many) and interview substitution (one person applying, another working).

Supported IDs: Passport, national ID, driver's license — 191 countries
Liveness: Active (prompted motion), not passive — harder to spoof
Review: 93% automated; 7% manual review within 4 hours
ID · live · 2025-11-08 14:23 UTC Capture
CEFR · speaking test Recording
Phase 02 · Skills

English test
(CEFR C1 required).

Every candidate completes a written + spoken English assessment graded against the CEFR scale. We don't test for perfection — we test for fluency. The bar is C1, which means "can work an 8-hour day in English without exhausting yourself."

The test is category-specific. Developers get a technical brief to respond to; VAs get a client communication scenario; designers get a creative direction to interpret.

Written: 600-word response, graded on clarity + grammar
Spoken: 4 recorded prompts, graded by human + automated pass
Reject rate: ~41% of applicants don't clear this phase
Phase 03 · History

Reference
verification.

Every accepted candidate provides two professional references — direct managers or long-term clients from the past 3 years. Our team contacts them by phone or video call, asks 6 standardized questions, and logs the response.

This takes 3–5 business days and is a real bottleneck. Candidates who provide fake references (it happens) are rejected and flagged across the network.

Required: 2 references from past 3 years, no personal contacts
Verified by: Live phone/video call with human Atlas team member
Fake-reference rejections: 3.2% of applicants · permanent ban
References · 2 of 2 Verified
● Continuous layer · active during every test

The anti-cheat stack.

During every skills test and video interview, four signals run in parallel. They're invisible to the candidate (aside from a camera/mic consent prompt), designed to catch the ways cheating actually happens — second monitors, off-screen assistants, AI ghost-answering, spoofed environments.

● Signal 01

Face match.

Live camera feed is continuously matched against the verified ID photo. A score below 92% flags the session for human review. Running at 2 fps the entire interview.

Threshold · 92% match
● Signal 02

Gaze tracking.

Pupil position is mapped continuously. Extended off-screen glances, repeated downward reading patterns, or synchronization with keystrokes all flag the session. Catches candidates reading from a second device or off-camera notes.

Flag after · 3 anomalies / min
● Signal 03

Audio analysis.

Every session audio is analyzed for second-voice events (someone whispering answers), breathing patterns that don't match speech (lip sync), and AI-synthesized voice signatures. Flagged segments go to a senior reviewer.

Detection · 3 overlap types
● Signal 04

Environment scan.

At session start, candidates rotate their camera 360° so we can confirm no second person in the room, no second monitor, and no off-camera assistance. A light passive scan continues throughout the session.

Coverage · 360° sweep + passive
● Symmetric · client side

Clients are verified too.

Candidates put themselves on the line to work with you. We hold you to a proportional bar: before you can hire, we confirm you're a real person or business with a real payment method. No anonymous clients.

Identity required.

Before your first hire, you verify either as an individual (ID + liveness, same as candidates) or as a business (registration documents + proof of authority). We check against sanctions lists and known-fraud databases.

Individual: government ID + liveness capture
Business: incorporation docs + authority-to-act proof
Screened against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists
Manual review within 12 hours if edge-case

Payment method
on file.

You add a verified payment method before your first hire — not at invoice time. A $1 authorization runs against the card or bank account to confirm it's real and open. If it fails, we don't let the hire proceed.

Card, ACH, SEPA, or wire — one must be validated
$1 auth is reversed immediately — you're not charged
Adding a backup method is optional but strongly encouraged
PCI DSS tokenized — Atlas never stores raw card numbers
● Money protection

Escrow, contracts,
and a 7-day window.

Every hour worked, every dollar paid, moves through a series of checkpoints. If something goes wrong at any point — missed work, overbilling, scope slip — there's a mechanism to pause, dispute, or reverse it.

01 · Contracts
Auto-generated

Every hire gets a real contract.

An Atlas-generated contract is auto-created at hire time, signed by both parties. Or, upload your own — our legal template covers IP assignment, confidentiality, termination, and governing law for 42 jurisdictions. Either way, no work starts without one.

42/42
Jurisdictions covered
02 · Escrow
Insured

Funds held in insured escrow.

The moment you're charged, the money sits in an FDIC-insured escrow account. It's released to the candidate 48 hours after your payment clears — never before. If a dispute opens, the relevant funds stay frozen until resolution.

48hr
Review window before release
03 · Hours
7-Day window

7-day hour
adjustment window.

You have a full 7 days to review, approve, or flag any hour a candidate submits. No auto-approve silently at midnight; we remind you twice, give you the evidence (time-tracker screenshots, activity log), and let you flag individual hours — not just whole invoices.

7 days
To review before auto-approve
04 · Disputes
Human mediator

Disputes go to a real person.

If you and the candidate can't resolve something yourselves, a trained Atlas mediator reviews the evidence from both sides and makes a binding call. Not an algorithm. Not an outsourced support tier. A named human on our team with an average 5–7 day turnaround.

5–7 days
Avg resolution time
● When things go sideways

The dispute flow.

Most contracts never see a dispute. But when one opens, you deserve to know exactly what's going to happen — how long it'll take, who's deciding, and what the possible outcomes are. No black box.

Dispute resolution · 5 stages
Avg total: 5–7 business days
Stage 01
Dispute opened.
Within 14 days
Either side opens via the invoice page. Reason + evidence attached at submit.
Stage 02
Both sides respond.
2–3 days
Mediator requests evidence from both parties. Funds frozen during this window.
Stage 03
Mediator reviews.
1–2 days
Named human at Atlas reviews both sides, may ask follow-ups, proposes resolution.
Stage 04
Outcome issued.
Same day
One of 4 outcomes: full refund, partial refund, no refund, or mutual settlement.
Stage 05
Appeal window.
7 days
Either side can appeal to a senior reviewer. Appeals rarely overturn but do get fresh eyes.
Who decides.
Three escalation levels. Each one is a named human, not a tier queue.
01
Dispute mediator
Atlas Trust & Safety team · trained in contract & payment disputes
Resolves 94%
02
Senior review panel
Two senior T&S leads · handles appeals & complex cases
Resolves 5%
03
Legal review
Outside counsel · reserved for jurisdictional or criminal-edge cases
Resolves <1%
● The plumbing

Data security
& privacy.

The details most platforms bury in a footer. Here's what happens to your data, where it's stored, and who can see it.

Encryption,
everywhere.

All data is encrypted both at rest and in transit, using industry-standard protocols. Payment data is additionally tokenized — we never store raw card numbers on Atlas servers.

At rest
AES-256
In transit
TLS 1.3
Payment data
Tokenized
Key rotation
90 days

Data handling
& retention.

We collect only what we need to run the platform and meet legal obligations. You can request an export or deletion of your data at any time through your account settings — no support ticket needed.

Minimization
Default
Retention
24 months
Export format
JSON + CSV
Delete on request
<72 hrs

Compliance
& audits.

We're audited annually by an external firm for SOC 2 Type II. We maintain GDPR compliance for EU users and CCPA compliance for California residents. Full reports available to enterprise clients on request.

SOC 2
Type II
GDPR
Compliant
CCPA
Compliant
Last audit
Q4 2025

Certifications
& standards.

Third-party audited. Reports available on request for enterprise clients.

SOC 2
Type II · renewed Q4 2025
GDPR
EU data residency · DPA available
CCPA
California compliance · do-not-sell

In progress · Q2 2026 target
● Direct line

Contact the
T&S team directly.

Suspected fraud, privacy request, security vulnerability, or a dispute that needs fast-tracking — this form routes directly to our Trust & Safety lead. 4-hour response on security issues during business hours.

// About Atlas · est. 2023

Talent is global.
Opportunity should
be too.

We built the hiring platform we wished existed when we were running teams ourselves. Real vetting, real humans, no cheating, no markup. A small pool of A-players, not a flood of pretenders.

See how it works
● The state of the industry

Hiring is broken.
Especially globally.

Every global talent platform we've used — and we've used most of them — suffers from the same three failure modes. The bar is in the basement. Here's what we saw, and what we do about it.

● Problem 01

A flood of unvetted candidates.

Most platforms will show you thousands of profiles the second you sign up. They make money on volume, not quality. The result: clients spend more time filtering than hiring, and the good candidates hide in the noise.

1/3
Freelancers self-report overbilling
● Problem 02

AI makes interview cheating trivial.

Second monitors with AI answer tools. Voice cloning. Pre-recorded clips played during live interviews. Without an actual anti-cheat stack, platforms are getting burned weekly — and clients never find out until the work starts.

4×
Reported interview fraud · 2023 → 2025
● Problem 03

Talent gets ghosted, banned, underpaid.

Candidates are a revenue source on most platforms — a 20% cut, arbitrary bans, opaque reviews, no recourse when things go wrong. The people doing the actual work are treated like ad inventory.

20%
Typical markup taken from candidates
● Our shift

Three opposite
bets instead.

Vetting by real humans — every accepted candidate personally reviewed. An anti-cheat stack that actually works — face match, gaze tracking, audio analysis, environment scan, all live during every test. Candidates keep 100% — the 10% fee comes from the client, because the client is the one buying.

That's it. No disruption, no revolution. Just three opposite bets on the same problem, and six quarters of compounding trust with the people on both sides of the marketplace.

● How we actually work

Five things we'll always do,
even when it's hard.

These aren't marketing values. They're the calls we keep making when a shortcut is available — and every one of them has cost us revenue at some point. We'd still make the same calls again.

01

Vetting over volume.

A pool of 10,000 unvetted people is worse than a pool of 500 A-players. We'd rather reject 95% of applicants than pad our numbers. Our current acceptance rate is 5.7% — and it's been going down as our brand attracts more applicants, not up.

● Cost: ~$3.2M/yr in rejected applicant volume
02

Humans in the loop.

AI scores. Humans decide. Every approved candidate is personally reviewed by one of our 11 recruiters, not an algorithm. When a client asks for a shortlist, a human assembles it. When a dispute opens, a human mediates it. We'd rather be slower than wrong.

● 48 hours to shortlist · avg · human-assembled
03

Transparency both ways.

Clients see everything about a candidate before hiring — work history, rate, video intro, anti-cheat flags if any exist. Candidates know exactly why they were rejected (with feedback), and exactly how they'd re-apply. No black boxes on either side.

● Every rejection includes written feedback
04

Talent pays nothing.

The candidate keeps 100% of their rate. Always. No listing fees, no "featured placement" upsells, no service cuts, no subscription tier to be visible. Our 10% fee comes from the client, because the client is the one buying. We don't extract value from the people doing the work.

● $0 charged to candidates · lifetime
05

Zero tolerance for cheating.

Our anti-cheat system is the strictest in the industry — four parallel signals (face match, gaze tracking, audio analysis, environment scan) running during every test and video interview. If someone games us, we catch it. The profiles you see are the people you're hiring.

● $0 in fraud losses reported in 2025
● The humans behind the platform

Founders.
In their own words.

Atlas was started by two operators who'd spent their whole careers hiring globally — and kept getting burned by the same platforms, over and over.

NR
Noor Rahimi
Co-founder · CEO
Noor spent 11 years building remote engineering teams — four at Stripe in their Ireland office, then at a Series B fintech where she grew the engineering org from 7 to 140. She watched every major global talent platform on the way, hired from most of them, and got burned by most of them too.
"I'd just rejected our 47th candidate in a quarter because the person in the interview clearly wasn't the person in the resume. And I thought: there has to be a better way to do this. So we built one."
DS
Diego Santos
Co-founder · CTO
Diego is an engineer from São Paulo who spent the 2010s applying to Silicon Valley companies — rejected 63 times before he got his first remote role. He then spent 8 years building platforms for distributed teams at GitLab and Doist. He builds the anti-cheat stack personally, and still sends every new engineer a printed copy of his original rejection emails on day one.
"If I'd had Atlas in 2013, my career would have started five years earlier. That's not a business plan — that's a debt we owe a lot of people."
● The rest of the team

All 28 of us.
Remote across 19 countries.

No office, no headquarters, no return-to-office debate. Filter by team to see who's in what function.

● Backed by

Operators who've built this before.

We took on a modest seed round in 2024 from a curated list of operators — people who have actually run global teams, not generalist funds. We wanted the kind of capital that comes with pattern-recognition instead of just a check.

Haystack
Seed · 2024
Calm Company
Seed · 2024
Remote First
Seed · 2024
Operator Collective
Angel · 2024
+14 angels
Ex-founders & CEOs
● Total raised: $4.2M seed · Default-alive since Q2 2025 · Not planning a Series A in 2026.
● Press & media

As seen in.

A selection of the coverage we've gotten since launch. We've generally declined interviews unless the outlet is actually interested in the model — so the bar here is higher than most "press" pages.

TechCrunch
The Verge
Fast Company
Wired
Forbes
Bloomberg
TechCrunch
Mar 2025
"Atlas is quietly rewriting how global hiring works — by saying no to 94% of applicants."
A rare platform that gets more selective as it grows, not less. The anti-cheat stack alone makes a case for paying the 10% premium.
Read article →
Fast Company
Jul 2025
"The Upwork alternative for companies who stopped believing in the unvetted marketplace."
Atlas's bet — that fewer, better candidates beat more, worse ones — is starting to look less like a pivot and more like the future of the category.
Read article →
The Verge
Nov 2025
"Inside the anti-cheat stack that's catching AI-assisted interview fraud before it lands."
The face-match-plus-gaze-plus-audio combination has detected 340+ fraudulent interview attempts in the last year. The industry should be paying attention.
Read article →
● Values

Five beliefs baked in.

Our values aren't on the wall, because there isn't a wall. These are the defaults we try to hire for and reinforce in how we work together.

● Value 01
Bar over bravado.
We'd rather have a small pool of real A-players than a big pool of pretenders. Applies internally too — we hire slow and fire slow, but we do both.
● Value 02
Transparency as a default.
If we'd be embarrassed to show it publicly, we don't do it. All salary bands are internally visible. All revenue numbers are internally shared. We default to open.
● Value 03
Talent deserves respect.
A-players on our platform are partners, not products. We treat them that way — fair rates, prompt payment, no surprise policies, and honest feedback when things don't work.
● Value 04
Build for the long game.
We're not here to flip this company. We're here to build the hiring platform our grandkids use. Every product decision gets a 10-year test before it ships.
● Value 05
Remote first, global always.
The best talent isn't in one zip code. Neither is our team. We hire where the person is, pay what the work is worth, and don't pretend locations matter more than they do.
● Value 06
Slow is smooth.
Smooth is fast. We're patient with the right things (vetting, hiring, product decisions) so we can move fast on the right things (support, disputes, fixing bugs).
● Two years, give or take

How we got here.

The short, honest version of the company history. The longer version is in our blog archives if you're curious about individual stretches.

Mar 2023
Atlas founded.
Noor and Diego meet at a remote work conference in Lisbon. Within 3 months, they've quit their respective jobs and are building the MVP from two different cities.
Sep 2023
Private beta · first 10 clients.
First real clients join the beta. All 10 still on the platform today. First approved candidate is a full-stack developer from Bogotá — who's still working with his original client.
Jan 2024
First 100 candidates approved.
Hit 100 approved profiles. Rejection rate crosses 92% for the first time. First recruiter joins the team full-time.
May 2024
Public launch.
Launched the platform publicly. Hit 1,000 approved candidates by end of Q3. The anti-cheat stack is now running on every session.
Aug 2024
Seed round closed — $4.2M.
Took on our first and only funding round. Operator-heavy cap table. No pressure to raise a Series A by a specific date.
Feb 2025
TechCrunch cover story.
First major press hit. Application volume 5× in 30 days. Had to temporarily pause new applications while we staffed up the recruiter team.
Jun 2025
Default alive.
Monthly revenue crosses monthly burn. Officially default-alive. We can run forever on what we're generating, and we'd rather grow slowly and keep it that way.
Today
28 team members · 2,699 approved.
8,214 successful hires. 68 countries. 11 recruiters. $0 in reported fraud losses in 2025. We're just getting started.

We hire globally. We believe in paying talent what they're worth. And we don't compromise on quality. If you've built something people still use years after you left, we probably want to talk.
