● Why work here

Six honest reasons.
Not eight. Not fifteen.

Most careers pages have a "10 reasons to work here" list padded with things like "great snacks." We've got six. They're real.

Reason 01

The mission is actually real.

Global hiring is genuinely broken — not in a "pitch deck problem" way, in a "we saw it from the inside for a decade and got burned by it" way. You'll be building toward an actual fix, not an incremental feature on top of someone else's broken marketplace.

● 2,699 lives changed · and counting
Reason 02

You'll work with actual A-players.

Every person on this team got hired partly because other people wanted to work with them. No B-players hired to pad a headcount number. No "the VP's friend's cousin." If you're tired of carrying mediocre teammates, that stops here.

● 9.4% internal hire acceptance · strict bar
Reason 03

Remote-first. Globally distributed.

Work from anywhere your tax and immigration situation allows. Async-first communication. Core overlap hours of just 3 per day, no 9-to-5 grind, no return-to-office debate. Salary is based on role value — not "local salary for local work."

● 19 countries · 21 timezones · 1 team
Reason 04

Transparent everything.

Salary ranges are published on every role below — no "DOE" or "competitive". Our internal salary bands are visible to the whole team. Equity structure is explained before you sign anything. No negotiation games; the stated range is the range.

● Internal comp doc · open to all staff
Reason 05

We actually invest in you.

$2,000/yr learning budget, no forms, no approvals under $500. Conference attendance covered. Books, courses, coaching — if it makes you better at what we hired you to do, spend the money.

● 94% of team used their full budget · 2025
Reason 06

Build something that compounds.

Small team means big ownership from day one. Your decisions shape the company. Your work compounds — we're not here to flip this company in 3 years, we're here to build the hiring platform our grandkids use. The work you do in year one will still be running when you're six years in.

● Default-alive · no Series A rush

● The hiring process

Five steps.
Typically 2–3 weeks start to finish.

Candidates hate unknown hiring processes. Here's ours — timing, format, and what each step actually means. No 10-hour take-home assignments, no ghosting, no "culture fit" vibes round.

Step 01
Apply.
10–15 min
Submit through the role page. Resume optional but welcome. Role-specific questions matter more than resumes for evaluation.
Step 02
Intro call.
30 min
Low-pressure conversation with someone on the team. You learn about us, we learn about you. Bring questions.
Step 03
Skills session.
60–90 min
Role-specific. Pairing for engineers. Portfolio walkthrough for designers. Role-play for recruiters. No trivia.
Step 04
Team conversations.
2–3 · 30 min
Short calls with the people you'd work with. Real work-style questions, not hypotheticals. Feels like a future coworker chat.
Step 05
Decision.
Within 1 week
We tell you yes or no within a week of the last conversation. No ghosting — if it's a no, you'll get feedback.
● Total timeline: typically 2–3 weeks · longer for staff+ roles Questions? careers@atlas.co
● Benefits & perks

With actual numbers.

Most careers pages say "competitive salary" and "flexible time off" and expect you to trust them. Here's what we actually offer, with numbers where numbers apply.

Transparent salary.
Published ranges on every role. Internal comp doc visible to all staff. No negotiation games.
Ranges are the ranges
Equity + profit-share.
Meaningful equity grants for every role. Profit-share kicks in once we hit profitability thresholds. Full structure explained before you sign.
0.05–0.5% typical
Remote anywhere.
Work from any country where your tax/visa situation allows. We'll help with contractor-of-record setup if needed.
19 countries currently
Async-first schedule.
Only 3 hours of overlap required per day with your region. No 9-to-5 grind. Meetings have a strict agenda or they get cancelled.
3 core hours / day
Learning budget.
$2,000/year for books, courses, coaching, conferences. No approval forms under $500. Use it or don't — we're not tracking.
$2,000 / yr
Home office stipend.
$1,500 one-time setup at hire. $500/yr refresh after that. Spend on chair, desk, monitor, whatever makes your workspace work.
$1,500 + $500/yr
Paid time off.
25 days PTO + local holidays. Explicit count, not "unlimited" (unlimited is often worse). Take your full allotment, we mean it.
25 days + local
Parental leave.
16 weeks, fully paid, gender-neutral. Available after 6 months on the team. Applies to adoption and fostering too.
16 weeks · all parents
Health coverage.
Full health insurance in countries where we have legal entities (US, UK, DE). Monthly stipend of $400–800 in other regions — documented per country.
Varies by country · documented
Annual offsite.
All 28 of us gather in person once a year. Costs fully covered — flights, hotel, meals. Past destinations: Mexico City, Lisbon, Cape Town. 2026 TBD.
1 week · paid in full
Mental health support.
$1,000/yr stipend for therapy, coaching, meditation apps — anything that supports your mental health. Separate from learning budget.
$1,000 / yr
● What we look for

Self-select in.
Or out. Honestly.

These are behavioral signals, not abstract values. Read them and either nod "yep that's me" or think "god no." Both are valid answers, and both save everyone time.

You write before you talk.
We're async-first. Most decisions happen in long-form documents with threaded comments, not real-time meetings. If you hate writing, you'll hate working here. If your first instinct when thinking through a hard problem is to open a doc, you'll fit right in.
You push back with reasons.
Disagreement is required. "I don't like it" isn't a reason. We expect people to push back on founders, leadership, each other — but bring a because. The best ideas here have survived two rounds of polite demolition.
You ship.
Shipping beats planning. We'd rather see your v0.1 in production than your v3 in a Figma file. Rough, real, and iterating beats polished, planned, and unshipped. Every time.
You're curious about people.
Half the job here — especially in recruiting, CS, support — is understanding what candidates and clients actually need, which is often different from what they say they need. That starts with genuine curiosity about humans.
You're in it for the long game.
Atlas is a decade-long project. We're not planning a Series A in 2026 and we're not planning an exit. If you're hunting for a quick flip or a fast-growing name to put on your resume, you'll be bored here. If compounding work sounds more interesting than hype cycles, keep reading.
● Equal opportunity

Diversity & equal opportunity.

We don't publish a statement we can't back up with data. Here's where we actually are, and here's where we're trying to get to. Specific over virtuous.

Where we are today.

Atlas doesn't discriminate in hiring on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or veteran status. We also think "we don't discriminate" is a low bar — we actively source candidates from regions and backgrounds historically underrepresented in global tech.

● Accessibility: Need accommodations during the hiring process? Email careers@atlas.co and we'll work with you. No shame, no process, no disclosure required on your application.
46%
Team identifies as women / non-binary
19
Countries represented on team
54%
Team based in Global South
14+
Languages spoken on team
● Don't see a role that fits?

Join our talent network.

We're always interested in A-players who want to help us build this. Tell us who you are and what you're good at — we'll reach out when a fit opens up, which is usually within 6 months.

Join talent network
● FAQ

Common questions.

The ones we get asked most. Click to expand. If yours isn't here, email careers@atlas.co — a real person reads every message.

Typically 2–3 weeks from application to decision. Faster for mid-level roles, longer for staff+. We aim to tell you something (even if it's "no" or "still reviewing") within 5 business days of your application. After the final conversation, you'll have an answer within a week.
We're remote-first, so visas usually aren't needed. Most hires keep working from wherever they already have legal right to work. If your situation requires visa sponsorship in a specific country, tell us in your application and we'll explore whether it's feasible for that role — but no guarantees.
Basically yes, with caveats. You need to have legal right to work in your country of residence. We need an employer-of-record option in your country (we use Deel for most places). Some sanctioned jurisdictions we can't hire into — we'll tell you upfront if your country is one of them. 90+ countries work today.
Meaningful equity grants for every role, vesting over 4 years with a 1-year cliff. Typical grants range from 0.05% (mid) to 0.5% (senior+). We use NSOs. Since we're default-alive and not aiming for a fast exit, equity is a long-game play — we explain this explicitly during the offer conversation. We also have a profit-share pool for roles where equity doesn't make sense (contractor-based geographies).
No. We don't have offices, so there's nowhere to relocate you to. You work from wherever you live. We'll cover home office setup costs so your workspace there works well.
Rarely, but not never. Our default is senior hires because we're small and everyone needs to be self-directed. That said — if you're junior-by-years but senior-by-work, apply anyway. We've made a few exceptions for people whose portfolios and track records were exceptional for their seniority level. Strong work speaks louder than YOE on a resume.
Yes — but pick the best fit first. Spamming every open role with the same application isn't a good signal. If you could genuinely see yourself in two roles, apply to the one you'd pick first and mention the other in your answers. Our team shares notes internally; we'll flag you for the second role if we see a fit.
You'll hear from us — no ghosting. If we interviewed you and you didn't get it, you'll get short written feedback on why and (with your permission) we'll keep your application for future relevant roles. We've hired several people on their second or third application.
$3,000 for successful referrals from outside the company, paid 6 months after the referred person joins (so we know the fit stuck). For internal referrals, we pay the same amount. To refer someone, use the "Refer someone" link on the talent network page.
Flat, with 2 layers maximum. Team leads report to our 4 function heads, who report to Noor (CEO). No middle-management empires. Individual contributors at senior+ levels have significant autonomy and typically work directly with function heads on scoping and prioritization.

● Who you are
5+ years building product.
Comfortable across frontend + backend · TypeScript, React, Go or Rust
You've shipped features end-to-end, not just tickets
Async-first by preference, not necessity
Writer. Pusher-backer. Person.
● Success in 12 months
You own a core surface.
Shipped 8–12 meaningful features in your first year
Trusted on scoping + architecture calls without oversight
Working proposal for your next Staff-level initiative
Hired or coached a more junior teammate
● About the role

The role.

You'll be our fifth engineering hire, joining a team of six senior+ engineers working across the Atlas platform. Most of your time will be spent on the client-side experience — the flows hiring managers use to browse candidates, build shortlists, message recruiters, and send offers. This is the surface clients live in daily, and it's where we can make the biggest impact on the quality of hires.

You'll report to Diego Santos (CTO) and work closely with Anjali Nair (Product Designer), Kenji Park (Frontend Engineer), and Tomás Núñez (Full-Stack). Most design-to-ship cycles are 1–3 weeks, not quarters.

"Our engineers pair with designers the way most companies pair engineers with other engineers. If you like shipping product, not just shipping code, this role will feel right." — Diego, CTO
● Responsibilities

What you'll do.

  • Own features end-to-end — from spec conversations with design and PM through production rollout. No "throw it over the wall."
  • Ship to production weekly. We deploy ~20× per week as a team. You'll have your own code live within your first 2 weeks.
  • Pair with designers daily. Most implementation decisions are made in real-time with design — not in a JIRA ticket after the fact.
  • Write production TypeScript + React on the frontend, production Go on the backend. We're pragmatic on frameworks — if we need Rust for a performance path, we'll use Rust.
  • Review PRs thoughtfully — we expect senior engineers to mentor through code review, not just approve or block.
  • Participate in on-call rotation (1 week every ~6 weeks). Most on-call weeks are quiet; we take reliability seriously.
● Must-haves

Who we're looking for.

Must have

  • 5+ years shipping production web applications
  • Strong across TypeScript + React (or similar — Vue/Svelte OK)
  • Comfortable with at least one backend language (Go, Rust, Python, Node)
  • A portfolio of features you owned end-to-end that are still in use
  • Excellent written communication — you'll be writing 2-page design docs, not just PRs

Nice to have

  • Experience at a marketplace or two-sided platform
  • Comfort with Go specifically (most of our backend)
  • Background in design or product — not just engineering
  • Worked remote-first for 3+ years
  • Open-source contributions of your own

If your experience doesn't map exactly to "5 years" or our stack, but you can point to features you've shipped that we'd recognize — apply anyway. We hire for trajectory, not just title.

● Stack

What you'll work in.

TypeScript React Go PostgreSQL Redis Temporal Tailwind Vite Playwright GCP Terraform Linear

We're pragmatic about our stack. We chose what we chose because it was the right call when we made it — and we'd change any of it tomorrow if a better call appeared. We expect you to do the same.

● Why this matters

Why this role matters.

The client-side surface is where the Atlas value proposition lands. Clients come in skeptical — they've been burned by unvetted marketplaces. The experience we give them in their first 48 hours determines whether they'll ever hire through us. This role sits on that conversion.

Everything from how a shortlist feels to review, to how natural a video intro plays back, to how obvious it is what the next step is — you'll own those details. And they compound. The 1% improvement you make to shortlist clarity in month three still pays off in month thirty.

● What we offer

What we offer.

  • $140–180k USD · based on experience and location calibration. The stated range is the range.
  • Meaningful equity · 0.1–0.25% for this role, 4-year vest, 1-year cliff. Full structure explained at offer.
  • Remote anywhere · 19 countries currently; we use Deel for employer-of-record where needed.
  • $2,000/yr learning budget · no forms, no approvals under $500.
  • $1,500 home office setup + $500/yr refresh.
  • 25 days PTO + local holidays + 16 weeks paid parental leave.
  • Annual team offsite · 2026 destination TBD.
  • See the full benefits page on the main careers page for details.
● Your future team

The four people you'd work with daily.

Small team, high overlap. If you join, these are the teammates you'll ship features alongside — most of your design docs and PRs will land in their inbox first.

DS
Diego Santos
Co-founder · CTO
You'd report to Diego. Ex-GitLab / Doist, originally from São Paulo. Builds the anti-cheat stack personally.
● Your manager
KP
Kenji Park
Engineer · Frontend
Most of the interactions on the current product were built by Kenji. Maintains two open-source libraries.
● Daily pair
TN
Tomás Núñez
Engineer · Full-Stack
Runs the dashboard experience end-to-end — profile through payments. Former pro chess player.
● Peer
AN
Anjali Nair
Designer · Product
Browse, profile, onboarding flows — mostly Anjali's pen. Fights for clarity over cleverness.
● Design partner
● What the interview looks like

Five steps. Tailored to this role.

Below is what you should specifically expect for the Senior Full-Stack Engineer role. Typically 2–3 weeks start to finish. No surprise rounds, no trick questions.

Step 01
Apply.
10–15 min
Form below. Share a project you owned and a short technical response. Takes <20 minutes.
Step 02
Intro call.
30 min
Call with Diego or a senior engineer. Mostly about your background and what you're looking for.
Step 03
Pairing session.
90 min
Pair with one of our engineers on a realistic problem from our actual codebase. No trivia.
Step 04
Design review.
60 min
Walk us through a past project with Anjali + Diego. Decisions you made and trade-offs you faced.
Step 05
Offer call.
Within 1 week
Decision call with the CTO. If yes, full offer — salary, equity, start date — all discussed here.
● Role-specific timeline: ~2 weeks for this role · 5 total conversations Questions? careers@atlas.co

Takes ~15 minutes. Fields marked with * are required; the rest are strongly encouraged but not blocking.

Thanks for applying.

We've got your application and everything attached. A real human on our team will review it within 5 business days — you'll hear from us either way.

Senior Full-Stack Engineer Ref #ATL-2045
● What happens next

Three things
we'll actually do.

No mystery timelines, no "we'll be in touch" ambiguity. Here's exactly what you can expect, and when.

01
We review your application.
A named Atlas teammate — not an algorithm — reads every field, opens every link, looks at every referenced project. We read cover letters in full. Your written answers matter more than your resume here.
Within 5 days
02
If there's a fit, we schedule an intro call.
30 minutes, video, low-pressure. You learn about us, we learn about you. Bring questions — the best intro calls are the ones where we both leave curious about the next conversation.
Week 1–2
03
You hear from us either way.
If we want to move forward, we'll schedule the next step. If we don't, we'll tell you, and we'll tell you why. Short written feedback for every applicant who interviewed. No ghosting. Ever.
Max 2 weeks
Total timeline 2–3 weeks from application to final decision. We track this metric internally and publish it quarterly on our blog.
● While you wait

Three things worth your time.

Since you've got a few days before we get back to you, here's the good stuff — the pages we'd want a thoughtful candidate to read before the intro call.

Page · Trust & Safety
The anti-cheat stack, in detail.
A deep tour of how we catch AI-assisted interview fraud — face match, gaze tracking, audio analysis, environment scan. The technical substance behind our pitch.
Read the full tour →
Blog · Engineering

Our async-first culture runs on written docs, not meetings. This post lays out how — the templates, the norms, the reasons. Good read before a round-two conversation.
Open the blog →

We hire across engineering, design, operations, and content. Some candidates end up a better fit for a role they weren't originally applying to — it happens often.

● While we're here

Know someone great for another role?

If you're reading this page, chances are you know other A-players who'd thrive here. Tell us about them. We pay referrers 6 months after the referred person joins — so both of you can be sure the fit stuck.

$3,000
Per successful referral
Paid 6 months after hire
Want to keep looking?
We have 7 other open roles, from Staff ML Engineer to Brand Designer to Customer Success. All salary ranges published.
Browse all open roles
Network open · reviewed monthly

Stay on our radar.

Skip the "keep my resume on file" charade. Our talent network is the real thing — reviewed monthly by our recruiter team, with personal outreach when a role opens that matches. Tell us about yourself once, and we'll do the rest.

● How this works

Not a résumé graveyard.

Most "talent networks" are just a polite way of saying "we threw your résumé in a pile." Ours is different — we actually review submissions, and we actually reach out.

01
We review submissions monthly — our recruiter team reads every one and tags them by interest area.
02
A recruiter reaches out when a role fits — personally, not with a template. Could be next week or in 6 months.
03
No spam. No list-building. We don't share your info with anyone, and we don't send newsletters.
7 hires
joined Atlas through the talent network — including 2 of our 11 recruiters. It's how we find the A-players we weren't actively looking for.

● Document library

Everything, organized.

Fourteen documents, four categories. Every document shows effective date, version, jurisdictional scope, and a one-line summary of the last substantive change. Past versions are archived — if you signed v3.2, you can still read v3.2.

01

Governing documents.

The documents that govern the overall relationship between you and Atlas. Every user agrees to these.

4 documents
Primary agreement All users
Terms of Service.

The foundational agreement governing use of the Atlas platform. Covers account obligations, permitted use, content ownership, fees, liability, termination, and dispute resolution including arbitration and class-action waiver provisions.

Last substantive change · Apr 18, 2026: Clarified scope of class-action waiver and added opt-out procedure for US users within 30 days of account creation. Expanded Section 12 (Limitation of Liability) to reflect SOC 2 Type II certification.
Effective Apr 18, 2026 · v4.2 · ~18 min read · Global
Read →
Conduct policy All users
Acceptable Use Policy.

What you can't do on the platform. Enumerates prohibited conduct including fraud, impersonation, scraping, and circumvention of the anti-cheat stack.

Effective Mar 02, 2026 · v2.1
Read →
Enterprise Clients · enterprise
Master Services Agreement.

Enterprise-grade alternative to Terms of Service for clients with custom needs. Supersedes standard ToS where executed. Available on request.

Template v3.0 · Negotiable
Request →
Status agreement Candidates
Independent Contractor Acknowledgment.

Candidates on Atlas are independent contractors, not employees. This document sets out that relationship, including tax implications and lack of employment benefits.

Effective Jan 15, 2026 · v2.0
Read →
02

Privacy & data.

How we handle personal data — collection, use, storage, sharing, and your rights. GDPR-aligned globally, not just for EU users.

4 documents
Core privacy doc All users
Privacy Policy.

The comprehensive account of what personal data we collect, why we collect it, how long we keep it, who we share it with, and how to exercise your rights. Structured by data category with a matrix at the top for quick scanning.

Last substantive change · Apr 10, 2026: Added new data category for biometric data processed by the anti-cheat stack (face-match vectors), with explicit retention schedule (90 days) and lawful basis (legitimate interest, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR). New candidate opt-out flow for non-hiring usage.
Effective Apr 10, 2026 · v3.4 · ~22 min read · Global · GDPR/CCPA/UK/LGPD
Read →
Tracking disclosure All users
Cookie Policy.

Every cookie we set, categorized by strictly necessary, functional, analytics, or marketing. Includes our cookie consent banner and preferences center.

Effective Mar 22, 2026 · v2.3
Read →
Art. 28 agreement Clients · controllers
Data Processing Agreement.

Pre-signed DPA available to any client acting as data controller. Incorporates SCCs for international transfers (2021 EU/EEA SCCs and UK IDTA).

Template v3.1 · Download
DPA + SCCs →
Vendor disclosure All users
Subprocessors List.

Every third-party vendor that touches user data, by category and location. Updated as processors change — with 30-day advance notice for material changes.

Updated Apr 05, 2026 · 11 vendors

Agreements that apply to specific user types or specific platform features. Most users don't need to read all of these.

Dispute Resolution Policy.

Read →
04

Compliance & governance.

Operational policies covering security, data retention, government requests, and vulnerability disclosure. Published here because transparency is table stakes.

3 documents
Security overview All users
Security practices.

Our SOC 2 Type II controls in plain language — encryption, access control, breach notification timelines, incident response.

Updated Mar 30, 2026 · SOC 2 on request

How long we keep each category of data, and why. Referenced by the Privacy Policy; published separately for clarity and auditability.

Effective Apr 10, 2026 · v2.4

Responsible disclosure Researchers
Vulnerability Disclosure.

Safe harbor for security researchers acting in good faith. Our disclosure timeline, scope, and bug-bounty structure.

Effective Feb 01, 2026 · v1.3
Read →
● Your data rights

What you can ask us to do.

Your rights depend on where you are — we've summarized the three primary frameworks below. For all users globally, we voluntarily extend GDPR-level rights as a floor, not just the minimum your jurisdiction requires.

🇪🇺 EU / EEA · GDPR

GDPR rights (the fullest).

  • Access (Art. 15) — ask for a copy of all data we hold on you
  • Rectification (Art. 16) — correct inaccurate data
  • Erasure (Art. 17) — "right to be forgotten," subject to legal retention
  • Restriction (Art. 18) — limit how we process your data
  • Portability (Art. 20) — receive data in machine-readable format
  • Object (Art. 21) — to processing based on legitimate interest
  • Withdraw consent — where processing is consent-based
  • Lodge a complaint — with your supervisory authority
Legal basis: Regulation (EU) 2016/679. Lead supervisory authority: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens, Netherlands.
🇺🇸 California · CCPA/CPRA

California rights.

  • Right to know — categories and specifics of personal info collected
  • Right to delete — request deletion, subject to exceptions
  • Right to correct — inaccurate information
  • Right to opt-out of sale/sharing — we don't sell personal data
  • Right to limit use — of sensitive personal information
  • Right to non-discrimination — for exercising these rights
  • Authorized agent — you can designate one to act on your behalf
Legal basis: Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1798.100–1798.199.100 (as amended by CPRA). Our "Do Not Sell/Share" link: in footer.
🌎 All users · Atlas policy

What we offer everyone.

  • Export your data — standard JSON format, any time, self-serve
  • Delete your account — full deletion within 30 days of request
  • Correct your profile — instantly from account settings
  • Opt out of marketing — one-click unsubscribe on any email
  • Access audit logs — view every time your profile was viewed, by whom
  • Challenge automated decisions — request human review of algorithmic outcomes
  • File a privacy complaint — to our DPO, with 30-day response SLA
Extended beyond any single jurisdiction's minimum. We treat UK, LGPD (Brazil), POPIA (South Africa), and PIPEDA (Canada) users under this framework.
● Submit a data rights request

Exercise a right, or just have a question.

Identity verification is required to protect your data — we'll ask for confirmation before acting. 30-day standard SLA; extensions only where legally justified and with notice.

Submit a request
● Subprocessors

Every vendor that touches your data.

GDPR Article 28 expects transparent disclosure of subprocessors. We publish ours without a sales gate, and we commit to 30 days' advance notice before adding a material subprocessor — giving controllers time to object.

11 vendors
● Current subprocessors · Apr 2026

All vendors vetted for SOC 2 Type II or equivalent certifications, bound by data-processing agreements incorporating SCCs for non-EEA transfers. Full list with locations, purposes, and retention terms in the published doc.

● Version history

Every substantive change, in the open.

We maintain a durable archive of every version of every document. If you signed ToS v3.2 on April 2nd, you can still retrieve v3.2 from us — your original bargain doesn't vanish just because we updated the page.

What counts as "substantive": any change that affects rights, obligations, fee structure, data handling, or legal exposure. Formatting and typo corrections are not substantive and are folded into the active version without notice.

How we notify users: for substantive changes to Terms of Service or Privacy Policy, we notify all affected users at least 30 days in advance by email, with a clear summary of what's changing and why. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance — but you can always close your account and export your data before then.

Need a past version? Every superseded version is archived indefinitely. Email legal@atlas.co with the document name and version number — we'll deliver a signed PDF of the exact text that was in effect on your date of interest.

● Who to contact

Named humans for legal matters.

Different questions go to different desks. Using the right inbox gets you to the right person faster — and gives us what we need to respond meaningfully instead of triaging.

● General Counsel
Elena Kapoor.

Contracts, commercial terms, enterprise negotiations, litigation, regulatory matters. 14 years prior experience at Latham & Watkins and Upwork.

legal@atlas.co
Replies within 2 business days · California Bar #289134

Johan Vermeulen.

Privacy inquiries, data subject requests, GDPR Article 37-39 matters. DPO certified (IAPP CIPP/E + CIPM). Based in Amsterdam.

● Governing law & disputes

Which law applies, which court decides.

The high-level summary. Full provisions are in Section 18 of the Terms of Service and Section 14 of each supplementary agreement. This summary is not a substitute for the operative text — if there's a conflict, the document controls.

Governing law.

US + rest of world
State of Delaware, USA, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles. Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. § 1 et seq.) governs arbitration clauses.
EU / EEA users
Dutch law applies to consumer users in the EU/EEA. Mandatory consumer protections of your local jurisdiction are not displaced.
UK users
Laws of England and Wales. UK consumer protections preserved.
Data protection
Each user's local data-protection law (GDPR, UK-GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, PIPEDA, POPIA) applies to their personal data regardless of governing-law choice.

Dispute forum.

First stage
Informal resolution. 60-day good-faith negotiation period required before any formal proceeding. See Dispute Resolution Policy.
Second stage
JAMS arbitration in San Francisco, CA for US users. Class-action waiver applies (opt-out available within 30 days of account creation).
Consumer exception
EU/UK consumers retain the right to sue in their local courts. Claims under $10,000 may also be pursued in small-claims court.
Injunctive relief
IP infringement and trade-secret claims are carved out from arbitration — either party may seek injunctive relief in court directly.
For the lawyers reading this: our Terms of Service use a forward-flow Callan-compliant severability clause. If any provision is held unenforceable, the rest survives — and the unenforceable provision is reformed to the maximum extent enforceable, not simply struck.
/ / Terms of Service
// Governing document · primary agreement v4.2

Status● Active
● Contents
● The short version

Plain-English summary.

Atlas is a marketplace connecting Clients (who hire) with Candidates (who work). When you sign up, you agree to behave honestly, pay fees on time, respect other users' IP, and not try to circumvent the platform to avoid our 10% fee.

Atlas is not a party to the work agreement between you and the person you hire (or the person hiring you) — the legal relationship is between them, as independent contractors. We facilitate the match, the vetting, and the payment flow.

For US users, disputes go through JAMS arbitration, not court — unless you opt out within 30 days of creating your account, or your claim qualifies for the consumer carve-outs. You also waive the right to file a class action. EU and UK consumers retain their local court rights.

This summary is for convenience only. The full policy below is the legally binding version. Where the summary and the full text disagree, the full text controls.

01Introduction & Acceptance

These Terms of Service (the "Terms") constitute a binding agreement between you and Atlas Talent, Inc. ("Atlas", "we", "us", or "our"), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business in San Francisco, California. The Terms govern your access to and use of the Atlas website, mobile applications, and related services (collectively, the "Platform" or the "Service").

By creating an account, accessing the Platform, or otherwise signifying your acceptance (for example, by clicking a box that says "I agree"), you represent that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms, together with any policies incorporated by reference — including the Privacy Policy, the Acceptable Use Policy, and any role-specific agreements. If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.

If you are entering into these Terms on behalf of a company or other legal entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity. In such case, "you" refers to that entity and its authorized users.

02Definitions

The following defined terms, when capitalized, have the meanings given in this Section 2 throughout the Terms. Other defined terms are introduced in context and capitalized on first use.

  • "Candidate" — a User whose account is categorized for the purpose of being hired for work, including after successful completion of the vetting process described in § 6.
  • "Client" — a User whose account is categorized for the purpose of hiring Candidates, whether an individual, sole proprietor, or legal entity.
  • "Contract" — a written or electronic agreement between a Client and a Candidate generated through the Platform pursuant to § 4.3.
  • "Content" — all information, text, images, video, audio, code, and other material transmitted, uploaded, posted, or otherwise made available on or through the Platform.
  • "Fees" — the Platform Fee and any other amounts payable under § 7.
  • "Platform Fee" — the service fee charged to Clients, currently 10% of Contract value, as described in § 7.2.
  • "User" — any person or entity with an Atlas account, including both Clients and Candidates.
  • "Services" (lower-case) — the work performed by a Candidate for a Client under a Contract.

03Eligibility & Account Registration

3.1Age and capacity.

You must be at least 18 years old and have the legal capacity to form binding contracts in your jurisdiction. Accounts registered in the name of minors are prohibited and will be terminated. If you are between 16 and 18 years old in a jurisdiction where you can legally work with parental consent, you may not use the Platform without separate written authorization from Atlas.

3.2Account information.

You agree to provide accurate, complete, and current information, including your legal name, email address, and (where applicable) tax identification number and payment details. You agree to update this information promptly if it changes. Submission of false or misleading information is grounds for immediate termination and may be referred to law enforcement where fraud is suspected.

3.3Account security.

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must enable multi-factor authentication when we make it available. You must notify us immediately at security@atlas.co upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access. Atlas is not liable for losses caused by unauthorized use of your account unless such use occurred through our gross negligence.

3.4One account per person.

You may maintain only one account. Creating a second account after termination of a prior account constitutes circumvention under § 12.

04The Platform; Role of Atlas

4.1What we do.

Atlas operates a two-sided marketplace that connects Clients with pre-vetted Candidates. Our services include: candidate identity and skills verification; profile discovery and matching; in-platform messaging; automated Contract generation; time-tracking and invoicing tools; payment processing through third-party providers; and dispute facilitation through our recruiter network.

4.2What we are not.

Atlas is not an employer, staffing agency, recruiter of record, professional employer organization (PEO), or payroll company. We are not a party to any Contract between a Client and a Candidate. Each Candidate is an independent contractor of the Client that engages them — not an employee of Atlas, and not an employee of the Client. The day-to-day working relationship, including scope, timing, deliverables, and performance, is between the Client and the Candidate.

4.3Contract generation.

When a Client and Candidate accept a proposal on the Platform, Atlas auto-generates a Contract that records the parties, the rate, the scope, the term, and standard protective provisions. Clients and Candidates may modify the standard Contract by mutual agreement, subject to our Acceptable Use Policy. Atlas is not a party to the resulting Contract and does not provide legal advice on its adequacy for your situation.

⚠ Worker classification risk · read carefully
Classification is the Client's responsibility.

Each Client is solely responsible for ensuring that its engagement of a Candidate complies with all applicable laws in its jurisdiction regarding worker classification, including — without limitation — the California ABC test (AB-5/AB-2257), IR35 in the United Kingdom, and any analogous tests in other jurisdictions. Misclassification can result in significant tax and employment-law liability. Atlas does not make classification determinations on behalf of Clients and is not responsible for the consequences of misclassification.

05Client Terms

5.1Scope of use.

As a Client, you may use the Platform to post hiring briefs, review Candidate profiles, initiate Contracts, manage active engagements, and process payments. You agree to comply with all applicable laws in your hiring practices, including anti-discrimination laws, equal pay laws, and applicable tax laws.

5.2Responsibility for the engagement.

Each Client is responsible for: (a) the accuracy of the engagement description; (b) verifying that work delivered meets its needs; (c) compliance with its internal legal, compliance, and export-control requirements; and (d) any regulatory obligations arising from its industry or the Candidate's geography (for example, data protection requirements for any data the Client shares with a Candidate).

5.3Non-discrimination.

Clients must not make hiring decisions on the basis of race, ethnicity, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristic. The Platform includes tools to help Clients evaluate Candidates consistently; use of these tools does not relieve the Client of legal responsibility.

06Candidate Terms

6.1Independent contractor acknowledgment.

As a Candidate, you acknowledge and agree that (a) you are an independent contractor and not an employee of Atlas or of any Client; (b) you are solely responsible for your own tax obligations, including income tax, self-employment tax, and any value-added or other transaction taxes in your jurisdiction; (c) Atlas does not withhold or remit taxes on your behalf; and (d) you are solely responsible for obtaining any licenses, registrations, or authorizations required to perform work in your country.

6.2Vetting and verification.

Candidates must complete Atlas's verification process, which includes identity verification, skills assessment, reference checks, and video-based interview rounds. You consent to recording of assessment interactions as further described in the Interview Recording & Biometric Consent Policy. See also § 13 (Privacy).

6.3Honesty in representation.

You must accurately represent your identity, skills, experience, and availability. You must perform your own work; you may not delegate assignments to third parties or use undisclosed AI-generated output as your own work product without the Client's knowledge and consent. Breach of this Section is grounds for immediate termination and forfeiture of pending payouts.

6.4Availability and withdrawal.

You may pause your account or withdraw your profile at any time through account settings. Withdrawal does not relieve you of obligations under active Contracts, which remain between you and your Client.

07Fees, Taxes, and Payments

7.1How we get paid.

Atlas charges Clients a Platform Fee. Candidates do not pay Atlas to use the Service or to be paid for work (except in narrow circumstances described in § 7.4). Fees are set out on the pricing page and may change as set forth in § 21.

7.2Platform Fee.

The Platform Fee is currently 10% of Contract value, calculated at the time of each payout from Client to Candidate and added on top of the Candidate's agreed rate. For example, if a Candidate bills a Client $1,000, the Client pays $1,100 ($1,000 to the Candidate plus a $100 Platform Fee to Atlas).

7.3Taxes.

All Fees are exclusive of taxes. You are responsible for all taxes imposed on your use of the Platform, except for taxes on our income. Where Atlas is required to collect VAT, GST, or similar transaction taxes on Fees, we will add them to your invoice. For US Candidates earning more than $600 in a calendar year from Atlas, we will issue IRS Form 1099-NEC in accordance with applicable law.

7.4Non-platform services paid to Atlas.

In narrow cases, Candidates may pay Atlas for specific services (for example, optional skills re-certifications, priority review, or premium profile features). All such charges are disclosed in advance.

7.5Payout timing.

Payouts to Candidates are initiated within two (2) business days after the Client's payment clears. Actual receipt depends on your payout provider and currency. First payouts to new Candidates may be held for up to seven (7) business days for compliance and fraud review.

08Escrow

Fixed-price Contracts may be funded through an escrow mechanism described in the Escrow Terms. Escrow is held by a third-party licensed escrow provider and is segregated from Atlas's operating funds. In the event of our insolvency, escrowed funds are not part of our estate.

09Intellectual Property

9.1Atlas IP.

The Platform — including its software, design, logos, trademarks, and other content created by Atlas — is owned by Atlas and protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws. We grant you a limited, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Platform for its intended purpose, subject to these Terms.

9.2Client-Candidate IP defaults.

The standard Contract template provides that, unless modified by the parties, intellectual property created by a Candidate in the performance of Services vests in the Client upon full payment, with standard exceptions for Candidate background IP and publicly licensed components. Atlas does not mediate IP ownership disputes beyond what is set out in the Dispute Resolution Policy.

10Content Licenses

10.1License to Atlas.

By submitting Content to the Platform — including profile data, work samples, ratings, and written communications — you grant Atlas a worldwide, royalty-free, sublicensable license to host, display, reproduce, adapt, and distribute such Content for the purpose of operating and improving the Platform. This license is perpetual to the extent necessary to retain historical Platform data and comply with legal retention obligations; otherwise it terminates when you delete the Content or close your account.

10.2Training of AI models.

We do not use your Content to train foundation AI models for public release. Our anti-cheat and matching systems may process your Content and activity for internal purposes, as further described in the Privacy Policy.

11Confidentiality

Information that is reasonably designated as confidential by one party, or that would be understood as confidential by a reasonable person (including Client project details, Candidate rates, and business plans), must be held in confidence by the receiving party and used only for purposes of the engagement. Confidentiality obligations survive termination of the Contract for three (3) years, except for trade secrets which are protected indefinitely.

12Non-Circumvention

● Non-circumvention · conspicuous clause
Don't go around us.

For a period of twenty-four (24) months following your first Platform-facilitated interaction with another User, you agree not to directly or indirectly solicit, employ, contract with, or pay that User outside the Platform for work of the type that the Platform supports. This includes attempts to move the relationship to off-platform payment channels, alternate email, or competing marketplaces, whether initiated by you or by the other User.

Permitted exit: a Client may convert a Candidate to direct employment or off-platform engagement by paying Atlas a conversion fee equal to 15% of the Candidate's anticipated first-year compensation, payable within thirty (30) days of the conversion. Written notice to conversions@atlas.co is required.

Violation is a material breach giving rise to liquidated damages equal to the conversion fee plus reasonable attorneys' fees, together with injunctive relief. We may pursue these remedies in court notwithstanding § 19.

13Privacy

Our collection, use, and disclosure of personal data is governed by the Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference. You have meaningful rights over your personal data, including rights of access, correction, and deletion; how to exercise them is set out in the Privacy Policy.

14Prohibited Conduct

Detailed prohibitions are set out in the Acceptable Use Policy, which is incorporated by reference. Without limitation, you must not: (i) misrepresent your identity or credentials; (ii) use the Platform to harass, discriminate against, or exploit other Users; (iii) attempt to bypass the anti-cheat monitoring applied to assessments; (iv) scrape, crawl, or extract data from the Platform; (v) reverse-engineer our software; (vi) interfere with the technical operation of the Platform; or (vii) use the Platform for any illegal purpose.

15Suspension & Termination

15.1Termination by you.

You may terminate your account at any time from account settings. Termination does not discharge obligations accrued prior to termination, including obligations under active Contracts and outstanding Fees.

15.2Termination by us.

We may suspend or terminate your account without prior notice if you materially breach these Terms, engage in fraud or abuse, or if required by law. For non-material breaches, we will typically notify you and allow a reasonable cure period.

15.3Effects of termination.

Upon termination: (a) your license to use the Platform ends; (b) any pending payouts will be released subject to our legitimate set-off rights; (c) you remain bound by surviving provisions, including §§ 10.1, 11, 12, 16–19; and (d) your data will be handled in accordance with the Privacy Policy.

16Disclaimers

● Disclaimers · conspicuous clause

THE PLATFORM IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE". TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, ATLAS DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

ATLAS DOES NOT WARRANT THAT ANY CANDIDATE WILL BE QUALIFIED, AVAILABLE, OR PERFORM SATISFACTORILY; THAT ANY CLIENT WILL PAY ON TIME OR IN FULL; OR THAT THE PLATFORM WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED OR ERROR-FREE. YOUR USE OF THE PLATFORM IS AT YOUR OWN RISK.

Some jurisdictions do not allow limitations on implied warranties, so some of the above may not apply to you. Where required, we provide the non-excludable warranties mandated by local consumer law.

17Limitation of Liability

⚠ Limitation of liability · conspicuous clause

TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL ATLAS, ITS AFFILIATES, OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, OR AGENTS BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, LOST DATA, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THESE TERMS OR THE PLATFORM, WHETHER BASED IN CONTRACT, TORT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR ANY OTHER THEORY, AND WHETHER OR NOT ATLAS WAS ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

ATLAS'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY FOR ALL CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THESE TERMS OR THE PLATFORM SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF (I) THE PLATFORM FEES YOU PAID TO ATLAS IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM, OR (II) ONE HUNDRED U.S. DOLLARS ($100).

Some jurisdictions do not allow the exclusion or limitation of liability for consequential or incidental damages, so these limitations may not apply to you. Nothing in these Terms limits liability for gross negligence, willful misconduct, fraud, or any other liability that cannot be limited by law.

18Indemnification

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Atlas and its affiliates, officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to: (a) your use of the Platform; (b) your breach of these Terms; (c) your infringement or misappropriation of any third-party right; (d) any Content you submit; (e) any dispute between you and another User; or (f) your violation of applicable law, including worker-classification laws. This indemnification obligation survives termination.

19Dispute Resolution; Arbitration; Class Waiver

⚠ Arbitration & class waiver · read carefully
For US users: binding arbitration, no class actions.

Except as provided below, any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to these Terms or the Platform shall be resolved by individual binding arbitration administered by JAMS under its Streamlined Arbitration Rules, before a single arbitrator, in San Francisco, California (or such other venue as the parties may agree). The Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. § 1 et seq.) governs the interpretation and enforcement of this arbitration agreement.

YOU AND ATLAS WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL AND ANY RIGHT TO BRING OR PARTICIPATE IN A CLASS, COLLECTIVE, OR REPRESENTATIVE ACTION. THE ARBITRATOR MAY AWARD RELIEF ONLY TO THE INDIVIDUAL PARTY AND ONLY TO THE EXTENT NECESSARY TO PROVIDE RELIEF WARRANTED BY THAT PARTY'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIM.

30-day opt-out. You may opt out of this arbitration agreement by sending written notice to legal@atlas.co within thirty (30) days of first accepting these Terms, stating your intent to opt out and including your account identifier. Opt-out does not affect any other provision of the Terms.

Carve-outs. Either party may (a) bring an individual action in small-claims court if the claim qualifies; (b) seek injunctive or other equitable relief in court for IP infringement, misappropriation of trade secrets, or breach of § 12; and (c) pursue a claim before an applicable regulatory authority.

EU/UK consumer carve-out. If you are a consumer domiciled in the EU/EEA or the United Kingdom, the arbitration clause does not apply to you to the extent prohibited by your local law, and mandatory consumer protections of your local jurisdiction are preserved.

20Governing Law & General Provisions

20.1Governing law.

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles. For EU/EEA consumers, the laws of the Netherlands apply. For UK consumers, the laws of England and Wales apply. In each case, mandatory local consumer protections are preserved.

20.2Severability.

If any provision of these Terms is held invalid or unenforceable, it shall be reformed to the maximum extent enforceable rather than simply struck, and the remaining provisions remain in full force and effect.

20.3Assignment.

You may not assign these Terms without our prior written consent. We may assign these Terms in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all of our assets.

20.4No waiver.

Failure to enforce a provision is not a waiver of that provision.

20.5Entire agreement.

These Terms, together with the documents incorporated by reference, constitute the entire agreement between you and Atlas regarding the Platform and supersede all prior agreements on that subject.

20.6Force majeure.

Neither party is liable for failure or delay resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, war, terrorism, pandemics, governmental action, or infrastructure failure.

21Modifications to the Terms

We may update these Terms from time to time. For substantive changes — changes that affect rights, obligations, fees, data handling, or legal exposure — we will notify affected Users by email at least thirty (30) days before the new version takes effect, with a plain-language summary of what is changing. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms. If you object to the changes, you may close your account and export your data before the effective date.

Non-substantive changes (formatting, typo corrections, clarifying non-material language) may be made without notice. The current version is identified at the top of this page, and prior versions are archived indefinitely and available on request.

22Contact

Questions about these Terms should be directed to legal@atlas.co. Notices required by these Terms to be sent to Atlas must be sent to:

Atlas Talent, Inc.
Attn: Legal Department
2261 Market Street #4218
San Francisco, CA 94114
United States

For data-protection inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@atlas.co. For law-enforcement requests, contact lawenforcement@atlas.co.

/ / Privacy Policy
// Privacy & data · core privacy document v3.4

Privacy Policy.

EffectiveApril 10, 2026
Last updatedApril 10, 2026
Versionv3.4
Applies toAll Users · global
Read time~22 min
Status• Active
• Contents
• The short version

Plain-English summary.

We collect data to run the platform — account details, profile info, work history, payment data, and (for Candidates) biometric data from ID verification and interview recordings. We process this data on well-defined legal bases (contract performance, legitimate interest, consent for sensitive categories).

We do not sell your personal data. We share it with subprocessors under Article 28 agreements, with other Users as needed for the marketplace to function, and with legal authorities when lawfully compelled.

You have rights: access, correction, deletion, portability, objection, and (for automated decisions) human review. Exercise any of them by emailing privacy@atlas.co — we respond within 30 days.

This summary is for convenience only. The full policy below is the legally binding version. Where the summary and the full text disagree, the full text controls.

01Introduction & Scope

This Privacy Policy (the "Policy") describes how Atlas Talent, Inc. (together with its subsidiaries, "Atlas", "we") collects, uses, discloses, and protects personal data of Users of the Atlas platform (the "Platform"), whether they are Clients, Candidates, or prospective Users.

This Policy applies globally. We extend GDPR-level rights as a baseline to all Users, regardless of jurisdiction. Where a specific jurisdiction provides additional rights or different requirements, those are addressed in the regional addenda at § 15.

02Controller Identity

For the purposes of the EU General Data Protection Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2016/679, "GDPR"), the UK General Data Protection Regulation ("UK-GDPR"), and comparable frameworks, the data controller is:

Atlas Talent, Inc.
2261 Market Street #4218
San Francisco, CA 94114
United States

EU Representative: Atlas Talent EU B.V.
Herengracht 123, 1015 BG Amsterdam, Netherlands

Data Protection Officer: Johan Vermeulen
Email: privacy@atlas.co

For California Consumer Privacy Act ("CCPA") purposes, Atlas is a "business" as defined in Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.140. For the Brazilian LGPD, Atlas is the "controller" (controlador).

03Information We Collect

3.1Information you provide.

When you register and use the Platform, you provide us with personal data directly, including: account information (name, email, password); profile information (work history, skills, rates, availability, photos); verification information (government-issued ID, proof of address); payment information (processed by our payment partners); Contract details; messages sent to other Users; and support correspondence.

3.2Information we collect automatically.

When you use the Platform, we automatically collect technical data: IP address, device identifiers, browser type, operating system, referring URL, pages visited, session duration, click patterns, and similar interaction data. We collect this data via server logs, cookies (see § 12), and our analytics tooling.

3.3Biometric data.

For Candidates, we collect biometric data from ID verification, liveness checks, and AI interview recordings. This includes facial geometry vectors used for identity verification and anti-cheat monitoring, voice-print features used to detect voice substitution, and gaze and pose tracking data used to detect off-screen reading during assessments. See the Interview Recording & Biometric Consent Policy and § 9 below for details.

3.4Information from third parties.

We receive limited information about you from: identity verification providers (Persona and Onfido); payment processors (Stripe Connect, Wise); anti-fraud services; and, for Clients, from references you submit to support your verification. We do not purchase contact lists.

04Data Categories Matrix

The table below summarizes the personal-data categories we process, the purposes, lawful basis (where GDPR applies), retention, and recipients. Where you see "legitimate interest," we have conducted and documented a Legitimate Interest Assessment (LIA) and are happy to share a redacted summary on request.

Category What Purpose Basis Retention
Account & profile Name, email, password hash, profile, skills, rates Operate the Platform; identify you Art. 6(1)(b) · contract Life of account + 12 mo
Identity verification Gov. ID, selfie, liveness check, DOB KYC, anti-fraud, compliance Art. 6(1)(c) · legal 5 years post-termination
Biometric data Face-match vectors, voice print, gaze data Identity & anti-cheat during assessment only Art. 9(2)(a) · consent 90 days from assessment
Assessment recordings Video/audio of interview rounds Scoring; integrity verification Art. 6(1)(a) · consent 180 days
Payment data Card tokens, bank details, transaction history Process Fees; payouts; tax reporting Art. 6(1)(b)+(c) 7 years (tax law)
Marketplace activity Proposals, messages, reviews, Contract history Facilitate marketplace; reputation Art. 6(1)(b) · contract Life of account + 3 yrs
Technical & usage IP, device, logs, clicks, session data Security, fraud detection, analytics Art. 6(1)(f) · interest 13 months
Communications Support tickets, in-app messages Provide support; improve Service Art. 6(1)(b)+(f) 3 years
Marketing Preferences, opt-ins, campaign history Newsletters; product updates Art. 6(1)(a) · consent Until opt-out

05How We Use Your Information

We use your personal data for the following purposes:

  • To operate the Platform — including authentication, profile display, messaging, Contract generation, payment processing, and support (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR: contract performance).
  • To verify identity and prevent fraud — KYC, anti-cheat, anti-impersonation, anti-money-laundering (Art. 6(1)(c): legal obligation; Art. 6(1)(f): legitimate interest in preventing fraud).
  • To match Clients with Candidates — using automated matching algorithms described at § 6 (Art. 6(1)(b): contract).
  • To improve the Platform — aggregated analytics on feature use, bug reports, and performance monitoring (Art. 6(1)(f): legitimate interest).
  • To communicate with you — transactional messages about your account, safety updates, and (with your consent) marketing communications (Art. 6(1)(b) for transactional; Art. 6(1)(a) for marketing).
  • To comply with law — including tax reporting, legal process, and regulatory requests (Art. 6(1)(c)).

We do not use your personal data for advertising based on your Platform activity. We do not sell your personal data (see also the CCPA addendum at § 15.1).

06Automated Decision-Making & Profiling

⚠ GDPR Article 22 · automated decisions
Where we use algorithms, you get human review.

We use automated processing at several points in the Candidate experience: matching Candidates to Client briefs; scoring assessment interviews; and flagging suspected anti-cheat anomalies. These outputs are used as inputs to human decisions by our recruiter team, not as sole grounds for adverse decisions.

Your rights. Where automated processing produces a decision that significantly affects you (for example, assessment failure leading to profile rejection), you have the right to: (i) obtain human review; (ii) express your point of view; and (iii) contest the decision. Contact privacy@atlas.co within 30 days of the decision and reference your Candidate ID.

07How We Share Your Information

7.1With other Users.

The Platform is a marketplace. Your profile is visible to Clients (or Candidates) as necessary for the marketplace to function. You control the level of detail shown. Messages and work deliverables are shared with the counterparty to your engagement.

7.2With subprocessors.

We engage third-party subprocessors to provide services on our behalf (hosting, identity verification, payments, email delivery, analytics). Subprocessors are bound by data-processing agreements that incorporate Standard Contractual Clauses for international transfers. The current list is published at the Subprocessors List, updated as vendors change, with 30 days' prior notice for material additions.

7.3With legal authorities.

We disclose personal data to law enforcement, courts, or other public authorities when we are legally compelled (subpoena, court order) or when we reasonably believe disclosure is necessary to prevent fraud, serious harm, or imminent threats to life. We narrow disclosures to the minimum data responsive to the request, challenge overbroad requests, and publish an annual Transparency Report.

7.4In business transfers.

If we are involved in a merger, acquisition, financing, or sale of assets, personal data may be transferred as part of that transaction. We will notify affected Users in advance where practicable.

7.5We do not sell personal data.

We do not sell, rent, or trade personal data for monetary or other valuable consideration, as those terms are used in the CCPA/CPRA. We do not "share" personal data for cross-context behavioral advertising.

08International Data Transfers

Atlas is headquartered in the United States and uses subprocessors in multiple countries. Where personal data is transferred from the EU/EEA, United Kingdom, or other jurisdictions with transfer restrictions to a jurisdiction without an adequacy decision (including, currently, most transfers to the United States outside the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework), we rely on:

  • EU Standard Contractual Clauses (Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914) — modules 1, 2, and 3 as applicable
  • UK International Data Transfer Addendum (IDTA) — as approved by the UK ICO in March 2022
  • Supplementary measures — including encryption in transit and at rest, access logging, minimization, and transfer impact assessments (TIAs)
  • EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework — for transfers to DPF-certified subprocessors

Copies of the executed SCCs and the DPA template are available to Clients acting as data controllers. Request at privacy@atlas.co.

09Biometric Data

⚠ BIPA & GDPR Art. 9 · biometric consent
We only collect biometrics with explicit consent.

Atlas collects biometric identifiers and biometric information within the meaning of the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act, 740 ILCS 14 ("BIPA"), for Candidates only, strictly for identity verification and anti-cheat integrity during assessments.

Purposes. Facial-geometry vectors match you to your verified ID photo across interview rounds; voice prints detect voice substitution; gaze and pose tracking detect off-screen reading. None of these are used for marketing, advertising, emotion analysis, or any purpose outside the assessment integrity use case.

Consent. Before collection, you are presented with a specific biometric-consent disclosure and must provide separate, affirmative consent. You may withdraw consent at any time, which will end biometric processing but may prevent you from completing additional assessments.

Retention. Biometric identifiers are retained for ninety (90) days from the date of the assessment, after which they are permanently deleted. This is more protective than the three-year ceiling required by BIPA § 15(a) and the GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) storage-limitation principle.

No sale; no disclosure. We do not sell, lease, trade, or profit from biometric data. We do not disclose biometric data except where required by law or where you have provided separate written consent.

10Retention

We retain personal data only as long as necessary for the purposes for which it was collected and as required by law. Category-by-category retention periods are set out in the matrix at § 4 and detailed further in the Retention Schedule. When retention expires or you request deletion, we securely delete or anonymize the data.

Some data is retained for legal reasons beyond account closure — for example, transaction records for 7 years under US tax law, KYC records for 5 years under anti-money-laundering law, or records responsive to a preservation notice from legal authorities.

11Your Rights

You have the following rights regarding your personal data, subject to certain exceptions under applicable law:

  • Access. Obtain a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
  • Correction. Correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
  • Deletion ("right to be forgotten"). Have your personal data deleted, subject to legal retention requirements.
  • Restriction. Limit how we process your data in certain circumstances.
  • Portability. Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format and have it transmitted to another controller where technically feasible.
  • Objection. Object to processing based on legitimate interest, including profiling.
  • Withdraw consent. Where processing is based on consent, withdraw it at any time (withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of prior processing).
  • Complain to a supervisory authority. Lodge a complaint with your local data-protection regulator.
  • Human review of automated decisions. See § 6.
  • Non-discrimination. We will not retaliate or discriminate for the exercise of any of these rights.

How to exercise. Email privacy@atlas.co with your request. We will verify your identity before acting to protect your data. We respond within thirty (30) days, with one extension of up to sixty (60) days where necessary and permitted by law, with notice.

12Cookies & Similar Technologies

We use cookies and similar technologies to operate the Platform and understand how it is used. Our Cookie Policy lists every cookie we set, its purpose, and its duration. You can manage your preferences through our cookie preferences center, accessible from the footer.

13Marketing Communications

With your consent, we send occasional product updates, newsletters, and feature announcements. You can opt out at any time via the one-click unsubscribe link in each email, or by updating your preferences in account settings. We comply with the US CAN-SPAM Act and EU ePrivacy requirements.

14Children's Privacy

The Platform is not directed to children under 18 years of age (see § 3.1 of the Terms of Service). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 13 (the age of digital consent under the US Children's Online Privacy Protection Act, "COPPA"). If we become aware that we have collected data from a child under 13, we will delete it promptly. Parents or guardians who believe their child has submitted information may contact privacy@atlas.co.

15Regional Addenda

15.1California (CCPA / CPRA).

If you are a California resident, you have the rights set out in § 11 as supplemented by Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1798.100–1798.199.100. In the preceding twelve (12) months, Atlas has collected the categories of personal information described in § 4 and disclosed such personal information to the categories of subprocessors listed at our Subprocessors List.

We do not sell or share personal information. We have not sold personal information in the preceding twelve months. Our "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link is in the site footer as required by law.

Sensitive personal information. We collect certain sensitive personal information (government ID, biometric data, precise geolocation in limited cases) solely for the purposes described in this Policy. We do not use or disclose sensitive personal information for purposes beyond those permitted under Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.121.

Authorized agent. You may designate an authorized agent to make a request on your behalf by providing written authorization and verifying your identity.

15.2EU / EEA (GDPR).

This Policy is designed to comply with the GDPR. Rights and legal bases are described throughout. Our lead supervisory authority is the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (Dutch DPA), on the basis of our EU main establishment in Amsterdam. You may also lodge a complaint with your local DPA.

15.3United Kingdom (UK-GDPR & DPA 2018).

For UK data subjects, all GDPR rights apply under the UK-GDPR as retained in UK domestic law. Complaints may be made to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).

15.4Other jurisdictions.

We extend comparable rights to Users covered by Brazil's LGPD, Canada's PIPEDA, Australia's Privacy Act, South Africa's POPIA, and similar frameworks. Specific requests are handled under the framework most protective of the requesting User.

16Security

We maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect personal data. Our security posture is described at the Security Practices document. We are SOC 2 Type II certified (as of March 2026) and undergoing ISO 27001 certification.

Breach notification. In the event of a personal-data breach that is likely to result in a risk to affected Users, we will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours as required by GDPR Art. 33, and affected Users without undue delay where high risk is indicated.

17Changes to This Policy

We may update this Policy. For substantive changes, we will notify affected Users by email at least thirty (30) days before the new version takes effect, with a plain-language summary. Non-substantive changes may be made without notice. The current version is identified at the top of this page; past versions are archived indefinitely and available on request.

18Contact & Data Protection Officer

For any privacy questions, to exercise a right, or to raise a concern:

Data Protection Officer
Johan Vermeulen (IAPP CIPP/E · CIPM)
privacy@atlas.co

EU Representative (Art. 27 GDPR):
Atlas Talent EU B.V.
Herengracht 123, 1015 BG Amsterdam, Netherlands

We will acknowledge receipt of your communication promptly and respond substantively within 30 days.

• Related policies
See also.
Tracking
Cookie Policy.

Every cookie we set, categorized and explained. Includes the cookie preferences center.

Read →
Vendors
Subprocessors List.

Every vendor that touches user data, by category and location. Updated with 30-day notice.

Read →
Data lifecycle
Retention Schedule.

Detailed retention periods by data category, referenced from Section 10 of this policy.

Read →
v3.4 · current
Apr 10, 2026

v3.3
Jan 28, 2026
Updated California CCPA/CPRA addendum to reflect sensitive personal information (SPI) limitations under § 1798.121. Added POPIA to regional framework coverage.
v3.2

v3.0 – v3.1
Q2–Q3 2025
